content:
I find it interesting that the degens on $RDDT are starting a viral campaign to save Wendy’s ( $WEN ).

The US fast food burger chain.

And the stock price is now up 20% overnight. 

Just for background: Wendy’s is a popular community meme, where people work behind the dumpsters after their portfolios go to 0 from 0DTE options.

(No positions, just found it amusing) 

I wonder if it’s going to work?

analysis.translation: 我觉得有意思的是，$RDDT的赌徒们正在发起一场病毒式活动，试图拯救Wendy's（$WEN）。这家美国快餐汉堡连锁店。股价一夜之间上涨了20%。背景：Wendy's是社区里的一个流行梗，指的是人们在0DTE期权爆仓后，去该公司垃圾桶后打工。没有持仓，只是觉得有趣。我想知道会不会成功？

content:
$MU reports tomorrow after the close and volatility is at 52 week highs. Let me break it down for you in long form:

The implied move is ~±11%. IV rank is pinned at 100. The expiry that catches the report is pricing implied vol near 177%, the back months sit closer to 95%.

  The internals are wild:
  • IV at 108% (30d) is a fresh 52-week high, the low was ~38%.
  • Realized vol is even higher, 128%, also a 52-week high, up from ~28%.
  • RV is running above IV, so the stock is moving more than the options price. That's often a negative variance risk premium, sitting at the bottom of its range. Into a print, that's unusual and worth respecting.

Hurst value around ~0.55, AR(1) ~0.98, mean-reversion half-life around 37 days. MU's vol is sticky. It clusters and fades slowly, so even after tomorrow's crush, realized likely stays hot for weeks.

On the earnings expiry, ATM IV sits around 177% and climbs as you move down in strike, so puts carry a premium over calls. 25-delta risk reversals are negative across the curve (about -1.7 vol points on the earnings expiry, steepening to roughly -5 by late July), and the deep tails balloon further. The tape is paying up for crash protection atm. With $MU's current 12% drop today, some call buyers are hitting OTM moves despite high vol.

Last seven reactions:  
• Mar '26: -4%
  • Dec '25: +10% (priced ~8%)
  • Sep '25: -3%
  • Jun '25: -1%
  • Mar '25: -8%
  • Dec '24: -16% (priced ~11%)
  • Sep '24: +15% (priced ~9%)

  Three of seven blew through the implied move, the rest did not.

  Bottom line into the print: IV and RV both at 52-week highs, RV above IV, vol that stays persistent with a vol smile tilted toward downside protection. Long premium needs a real move just to beat the crush. Short premium banks the rich IV but is short the gap that's torched sellers before.

  Full smile, term structure, skew, past straddles and every prior reaction:
  https://t.co/qgejDMLOUa
  https://t.co/PttlZVRUoa

analysis.summary: 该推文分析了美光科技（MU）财报前的期权市场数据，指出隐含波动率创52周新高，实际波动率高于隐含波动率，且偏斜显示市场倾向于买入看跌期权进行下行保护，历史数据显示7次财报中有3次突破隐含波动率，整体偏空。
analysis.translation: $MU明天收盘后发布财报，波动率处于52周高点。让我详细分析：隐含波动约为±11%，IV排名100。财报周期的隐含波动率约177%，远月约95%。内部数据异常：30天IV为108%（52周新高，低点约38%），实际波动率128%（也创新高，低点约28%），RV高于IV，表明股票波动超过期权定价，负方差风险溢价处于底部，财报前不常见。Hurst值约0.55，AR(1)约0.98，均值回归半衰期约37天，MU的波动率有粘性，缓慢衰减，即使财报后下降，实际波动率可能持续数周。财报到期日的ATM IV约177%，向下行权价攀升，看跌期权溢价高于看涨。25-delta风险逆转在曲线内为负（财报周期约-1.7个波动率点，到7月下旬加深至约-5），远端尾部进一步扩大，市场正在为崩盘保护支付溢价。MU今日下跌12%，一些看涨期权买家仍在高波动下买入OTM期权。过去7次财报反应：2026年3月-4%，2025年12月+10%（隐含约8%），2025年9月-3%，2025年6月-1%，2025年3月-8%，2024年12月-16%（隐含约11%），2024年9月+15%（隐含约9%）。7次中有3次突破隐含波动，其余未突破。结论：IV和RV均处于52周高点，RV高于IV，波动率持久，偏斜向下行保护倾斜。买入溢价需要大幅波动才能对冲降波；卖出溢价获得高波动率收益，但面临过去多次爆仓的风险。完整微笑曲线、期限结构、偏斜、过往跨式策略及每次反应见链接。

content:
Swing/recovery trading is always a fun side hobby. If I had to make a "beaten down" shower thought list in the US rn:

- $CRCL $78: Rate hikes actually benefit this name, down from $132 -> $78.
- $NFLX $71 - Down 41.6% over the past year. Probably just no banger shows like Squid Games, but keeps growing. 
- $ETHA / $IBIT $1.65k / $62k- Always a fan of Ethereum in the $1500-$1700 range. Also like Bitcoin around here. 
- $ASTS $75- just from a swing trading perspective always seems to recover from $70, just a matter of time
- $META / $MSFT $566 / $372 - two of the mag7 beaten down a lot from capex, but both growing remarkably fast. 
- $UPWK $8 - might be time to revisit this name, P/E ratios are extremely low, and I still think people will need to hire... which makes the economy go round
- $RDDT - probably CSP around $150 might be ideal when it's $170 now

Would probably be the ones I like. AI buildout is just going to keep rising into 2028, so I'm still bottleneck/chokepoint investing, but just throwing out more normal ideas. 

For US AI names I'd personally buy at current levels maybe,

$AAOI at $150 is super compelling to me personally. 
$JBL at $370 ($39B) is also very compelling. 

maybe $TSM is a sleeper at $1.9T if they're price hiking now. There's been reports around 2% margin increases or so from 10%? price hikes.

Will need to look more into $AXTI new float mechanics before I talk about it more. Lot of my older ideas like $TSEM, $VPG, $COHR, $LITE, $AEHR, $VPG, $NBIS already got re-rated hundreds of percent so it's not quite clearly undervalued compared to before but still upside. 

I'm still holding onto $EWY and memory exposure, but personally not adding more. 

Some of the others I'd personally sell CSP on since $INTC, $MRVL, and others seem priced about right now given premiums if i had no positions? and IV is extremely high across the board.

analysis.summary: 作者认为当前美股部分股票和加密货币处于超卖或低估状态，列举了多个看好的标的，包括受益于加息的公司、大型科技股、加密货币和AI相关个股，整体立场偏向看涨。
analysis.translation: 波动/复苏交易一直是个有趣的副业。如果让我现在在美国列一个“被打压”的沉思清单：$CRCL $78：加息实际上对该股有利，从$132跌至$78。$NFLX $71 - 过去一年下跌41.6%。可能只是没有像鱿鱼游戏那样的火爆节目，但仍在增长。$ETHA / $IBIT $1.65k / $62k - 一直看好以太坊在$1500-$1700区间。也喜欢现在价位的比特币。$ASTS $75 - 仅从波段交易角度看，似乎总能从$70反弹，只是时间问题。$META / $MSFT $566 / $372 - 两只因资本开支而大幅下跌的M7股票，但两者都增长非常快。$UPWK $8 - 可能是时候重新关注了，市盈率极低，我仍然认为人们需要雇佣……这让经济运转。$RDDT - 可能当价格在$170时，行权价$150的现金担保看跌期权是理想的。这些可能是我喜欢的。AI建设将持续增长到2028年，所以我仍在瓶颈/咽喉点投资，但只是提出更多常规想法。对于美国AI股票，我个人会在当前水平买入的或许是$AAOI $150对我来说非常有吸引力。$JBL $370（$390亿）也很有吸引力。也许$TSM是$1.9万亿的黑马，如果现在他们在提价。有报道说利润率可能从10%提高约2个百分点。需要更多了解$AXTI的新的流通机制后再详细讨论。我很多旧想法如$TSEM、$VPG、$COHR、$LITE、$AEHR、$VPG、$NBIS已经被重估了几百个百分点，所以不像以前那样明显低估，但仍有上涨空间。我仍然持有$EWY和内存相关头寸，但个人不会增加。其他一些我倾向于卖现金担保看跌期权的包括$INTC、$MRVL等，如果我没有头寸的话，现在看起来定价合理？而且隐含波动率普遍极高。

"The «Fab4Micro» in Erfurt is a key project for the technological sovereignty of Germany and Europe, explained Thuringia’s head of government."

The production start is planned in 2028 (finanzen). 

With Europe's new CHIPS ACT 2 as a concurrent fundamental tailwind.

analysis.translation: 今天，市值12.3亿美元的赛微电子（$XFAB）从欧盟芯片法案中获得1.274亿欧元，用于其MEMS代工厂。图林根州政府首脑表示：“位于爱尔福特的‘Fab4Micro’是德国和欧洲技术主权的关键项目。”投产计划于2028年（据finanzen报道）。而欧洲新芯片法案2.0则作为同步的长期利好因素。

Since we got a massive drop off BS narratives like 3 rate hikes off no newly material macroeconomic data (which comes out Thursday). 

If institutions really believed 3 rate hike sellside garbage that BofA put out:

They would profit off it with CME/prediction markets. 

Which are still projecting 74% no rate hike in July. But they don't. 

So they're feeding retail investors garbage.

And framing Taiwan’s $TSM ~23.6x forward p/e and $NVDA upstream supply chain as a “bubble”.

analysis.summary: 该推文批评彭博社将台积电和英伟达上游供应链比作泡沫，认为这是愚蠢的分析，暗示这些资产被低估或市场过度悲观。推文维护韩国KOSPI指数，并指责媒体使用误导性词汇（如“债务”代替“保证金”）削弱了零售投资者的信任。整体上，推文对相关科技股持看涨态度。

content:
Advantest (TSE: 6857 / $ATEYY) × OpenLight: Silicon Photonics HVM Test Partnership

Advantest and OpenLight have announced a partnership to co-develop scalable, high-volume manufacturing (HVM) test solutions for silicon photonics (SiPh) devices. The demand logic runs cleanly through the release: expanding AI/HPC workloads drive data-center adoption of SiPh and co-packaged optics (CPO), which in turn surfaces a production-stage test bottleneck. That bottleneck is the high-volume testing of electro-optical (EO) devices, specifically the Optical Engine. SiPh devices demand simultaneous electrical measurement and optical coupling/alignment, so conventional electrical ATE infrastructure alone cannot deliver the throughput needed to scale. The two companies target an end-to-end test framework spanning wafer sort through final test, with OpenLight contributing its III-V heterogeneous integration PASIC (Photonic ASIC) and PDK, and the release citing a recent $50M Series A-1 round (total funding $84M) as evidence of commercial momentum.

Advantest enters SiPh test directly, sharpening the ATE duopoly contest. This move puts Advantest in head-to-head positioning against Teradyne's Photon 100 in the EO/tester layer, with both incumbents now moving to absorb production-test demand from fabless PIC design houses like OpenLight.

The constraint is structural, not incremental. Combining electrical test with optical alignment at volume is the hard part; framing it as "end-to-end, wafer sort to final test" signals the partners are targeting the full insertion chain rather than a single station, which is where throughput economics actually break.

Read against the "Three Pillars" frame, this is an ATE-side consolidation signal. It complements rather than displaces the AEHR/FORM/KEYS wafer-test framework (https://t.co/XvOhml7Zok), but the specific division of labor (optical alignment method, WLBI applicability, insertion architecture) is unstated and rests on a promotional first-party release, so it warrants cross-checking against later conference disclosures.

analysis.summary: 爱德万测试与OpenLight合作开发硅光子大规模测试方案，解决AI/HPC驱动下的光电测试瓶颈，强化ATE双头垄断格局，利好爱德万测试。
analysis.translation: 爱德万测试（TSE: 6857 / $ATEYY）宣布与OpenLight合作，共同开发用于硅光器件的高量产测试解决方案。随着AI/HPC负载扩张，数据中心采用硅光子和共封装光学，生产阶段的光电测试瓶颈凸显。该合作瞄准电光器件（尤其是光学引擎）的高产测需求，提供从晶圆分选到最终测试的端到端框架。OpenLight贡献其III-V异质集成PASIC和PDK，近期5000万美元A-1轮融资显示商业动能。此举使爱德万测试与泰瑞达在电光测试层直接竞争，两家巨头正吸收来自OpenLight等无晶圆PIC设计公司的产测需求。该约束是结构性的，而非增量式。

content:
IMO photonics theme + CW laser chokepoint is goated. 

It's legit like markets have short term memory loss and forgot how $LITE went from $3B -> $65B+ from 2024 to now.

Because $NVDA caused EML bottlenecks, and forced architectural changes. 

We're literally seeing the same thing today with CW lasers + 1.6T/CPO shifts with Nvidia signing LTAs everywhere.

Now, $AMD + other CSPs are hunting for remaining scraps with large LTAs for CW lasers + optical components. 

GS Research's ~9-10x $154B optical TAM in 2028 and near $0 -> $91B CPO TAM in just 2 1/2 years.

Don't just magically disappear from a month of trading volatility. 

$AAOI sitting at ~$13B, $SIVE sitting at ~$3B, and other CW laser players look strategically very valuable. 

And next year I think we'll look back and say "Why didn't I learn my lesson the first time with EML from Nvidia and pick up CW laser adjacent names!" 

Then there's likely gonna be some new mini trend 1-2 years from now like microled or quantum dot and we're gonna see the same thing repeat. 

Think Sumitomo's projections with CW laser share + silicon photonics being majority / dominant architecture should be correct. 

I'm personally just focusing on that bottleneck as you've seen with $SOI, $TSEM, $SIVE, and others.

analysis.summary: 该推文看好光子学主题，特别是连续波激光器领域的瓶颈机会，认为当前市场低估了相关股票（如AAOI、SIVE等），并类比此前NVDA推动EML带来的暴涨，预测连续波激光器和硅光子学将主导未来架构，建议投资者抓住类似机会。
analysis.translation: 我认为光子学主题加上连续波激光器的瓶颈非常看好。市场就像有短时记忆，忘记了$LITE在2024年到现在的市值从30亿涨到650亿以上。因为$NVDA导致EML瓶颈，迫使架构改变。如今我们同样看到连续波激光器和1.6T/CPO的转变，Nvidia到处签署长期协议。现在$AMD和其他云服务商也在争抢剩余资源，为连续波激光器和光学组件签署大型长期协议。高盛研究预测2028年光学TAM约1540亿美元，增长9-10倍，而CPO TAM在两年半内从近0到910亿美元。不要因为一个月的交易波动就消失。$AAOI约130亿市值，$SIVE约30亿市值，其他连续波激光器玩家看起来战略价值很高。明年我们可能会回望并说'为什么我没从上次NVDA的EML教训中学到，而提前布局连续波激光器相关标的？'然后一两年后可能又会出现新的微型趋势，如microled或量子点，类似情况会重演。我认为住友关于连续波激光器份额和硅光子学成为主导架构的预测应该正确。我个人专注于那个瓶颈，正如你们看到的$SOI、$TSEM、$SIVE等。

content:
I actually think OE Solutions ($138080.KQ
) makes the most sense as an acquisition target by a large downstream player.

Since it’s now trading at a stupid ~$215M MC for scarce EML/CW laser IP.

A Source Photonics / Suzhou Dongshan Precision type acquisition might add a lot more value to another company that wants to vertically integrate. 

Given there’s a lot of expensive R&D and capex required to scale their fabs and buildout ahead + it’s a small optical player. (Disclosure: I do own positions)

Just floating the idea out there to other companies.

analysis.summary: 作者认为OE解决方案（138080.KQ）作为下游大型企业的收购目标非常合理，尽管市值仅约2.15亿美元，但其稀缺的EML/CW激光IP价值被低估。类似Source Photonics或东山精密的收购案例可能为希望垂直整合的公司带来更大价值。由于该小型光通信企业需要大量研发和资本支出以扩建晶圆厂，作者提出这一想法供其他公司参考（作者持有仓位）。
analysis.translation: 我实际上认为OE解决方案（138080.KQ）作为下游大型企业的收购目标最合理。该股目前以约2.15亿美元的愚蠢市值交易，却拥有稀缺的EML/CW激光IP。类似Source Photonics或苏州东山精密的收购类型，可能为希望垂直整合的其他公司增加更多价值。考虑到扩大其晶圆厂和前期建设需要大量昂贵的研发和资本支出，且它是一家小型光通信企业。（披露：我持有仓位）只是把这个想法提出来供其他公司参考。

content: @aleabitoreddit 你说的这些AI股涨的前提是Google/Microsoft/Amazon这种hyperscaler继续加大投资，但是当他们的利润全都被半导体公司赚走的时候还有会动力加大资本支出吗？现在这个趋势其实对后续股市非常危险，当hyperscaler不再愿意无限扩大支出，又或者anthropic营收涨幅不及预期的话股市很容易一夜崩盘。

analysis.summary: 推文指出AI股上涨依赖超大规模企业持续投资，但如果利润被半导体公司赚走，这些企业可能失去扩大资本支出的动力，导致市场风险增加。一旦超大规模企业停止投资或AI公司营收不及预期，股市可能崩盘。
analysis.translation: 你提到的这些AI股上涨的前提是Google、Microsoft、Amazon这类超大规模企业继续加大投资，但是当它们的利润全都被半导体公司赚走的时候，还会有动力增加资本支出吗？现在的趋势对后续股市非常危险，当超大规模企业不再愿意无限扩大支出，或者anthropic营收涨幅不及预期时，股市很容易一夜崩盘。

content:
You've heard of the 1000% gains, but do you know the history?

Unusual Whales focuses on options.

But how does it work with stocks like SpaceX, $SPCX, and GameStop, $GME?

Take a listen. https://t.co/O3UJuGBo0w

analysis.summary: 推文提及SpaceX（$SPCX）和GameStop（$GME）股票，以及Unusual Whales的期权交易策略，并邀请收听相关讨论，但未明确表达看涨或看跌观点。

content:
That’s for you to decide.

But just throwing this out there:

If $AAOI hits projections, that’s over 800%+ Y/Y growth, and $5.6B revenue annualized off $12.8B MC.

My guess is that the $471m/month will keep going up as demand seems to scale exponentially into 2028.

I’d expect ASP/margins to go up across the board as well. Especially as the industry is bottlenecked and $AMD + CSPs are going around hunting for LTAs.

content:
As for $LPK: Maybe $3B-$5B seems reasonable when they fully volume ramp if I had to guess. 

Feels more asymmetrical to me personally since it's just a waiting game and they have the customers for glass substrates. 

Small machine supplier chokepoints usually cap out from TAM though and don't go to $20B+ unless you're ASML or hold many chokepoints like KLAC. 

Not many dominant "monopolies" like these out there right before volume ramp this small: 

Maybe you have stuff like: 
- Aixtron (MOCVD) ~$8B
- Towa (compression bonding) ~$2B 
- Techwing (memory handler/cube probes) ~$1.5B
- MSScorps (CPO inspection), but pre-ramp. $850M
- Riber (quantum MBE), very pre-ramp ~$350M 

Then LPK Laser for glass core substrates (about to ramp) at ~$730M, which thematically should have more premiums than memory. (eg. major advanced packaging shift, CPO adjacent, etc). 

Lot of obscure chemical monopolies I know of, but those don't get as much attention cause BOM is much lower than equipment. 

But for lpk you have:

eg. “80% of customers among major global players have selected LPKF equipment”.  

70% of LIDE market share target for TGV in the glass-core ramp, should be very material. (disclosure: own, the listed names above aside from techwing/aixtron, NFI).

Just kinda the path they go if you follow $AEHR at ~$3.5B now:

- <$500m: "Oh they have no customers!" 
---> (probably somewhere in the middle here).
- < $1.5B: "Maybe volume comes soon!" 
- $3B+ onward: "Looks like they're starting to volume ramp. 

Just a lesson for the cycle of the chokepoint machine suppliers from my personal experiences.

analysis.summary: 作者看好LPK作为玻璃基板光刻设备的潜在垄断供应商，认为其当前市值（约7.3亿美元）被低估，随着量产启动，市值有望达到30-50亿美元。对比类似设备垄断公司（如Aixtron、Towa）的市值增长路径，并引用客户占有率数据支撑观点。
analysis.translation: 关于$LPK：如果全面量产，我猜测合理市值可能在30亿到50亿美元之间。我个人感觉它的风险回报更不对称，因为只需要等待，而且他们已经有玻璃基板的客户。小型设备供应商通常受限于总可寻址市场，市值难以突破200亿美元，除非你是ASML或像KLAC那样拥有多个瓶颈环节。像这样在量产前夕、市值如此小的潜在垄断公司并不多：例如，Aixtron（MOCVD）约80亿美元，Towa（压缩键合）约20亿美元，Techwing（内存处理器/立方体探针）约15亿美元，MSScorps（CPO检测）但尚未量产，市值8.5亿美元，Riber（量子MBE），非常早期，约3.5亿美元。而LPK激光用于玻璃基板（即将量产），市值仅7.3亿美元，主题上应该比存储器有更高溢价（例如，重大先进封装转型、CPO相关等）。我还知道很多小众化学垄断企业，但它们不太受关注，因为材料成本远低于设备。例如，LPK：'全球主要客户中有80%选择了LPKF设备'。玻璃基板量产中，TGV的LIDE市场份额目标为70%，这将非常可观。（披露：持有LPK，以及上述除Techwing/Aixtron外的公司，非投资建议）。这有点像他们走的路，可以参考$AEHR当前约35亿美元市值：低于5亿美元时：'哦，他们没客户！' ——>（可能我们现在就处于中间某处）。低于15亿美元时：'也许量产快到了！' 超过30亿美元后：'看起来他们开始量产了。' 这是我个人经历中关于瓶颈设备供应商市值周期的一个教训。

content:
Wow, 3 limit ups in a row with WUS TW. Pretty sad I didn't take larger positions. 

WUS TW is 1 my 2 NAV arbitrage + independent growth trades. 

If you want context: WUS TW is a ~$1.12B PCB player with AI DC growth. 

Their stake in WUS Kunshan is $4.79B (11.4% of 42.04 billion)

So basically they're a $1.1B PCB player growing independently, sitting on ~$4.79B worth of another company that looks to IPOed on Hong Kong markets soon. 

And a successful activist investor in Palliser (with Ajinomoto and Toto), is pressuring them to trim some holdings along with a few others. 

Seems like a pretty insane disconnect, and I think there's a lot of potential here.

content:
That’s a misconception:

$SIVE is the laser supplier for next gen architectures, not just CPO scale up. 

Pluggable, scale out CPO, scale up CPO, NPO, etc. 

- Sivers and $JBL went god-mode and developed 1.6T optical transceivers with CW lasers.

Effectively designing around the EML bottlenecks, and even made things more power efficient. 

From quotes from Jabil management they created a “relatively dramatic moat”.

So for the next gen of 1.6T pluggable transceivers, Sivers seems immediately used.

Markets missed this nuance too: after Jabil’s announcement, other pluggable players reached out, and SIVE is working with them now (prob codevelopment, qualification stage).

There’s not too many players that build optical transceivers that aren’t vertically integrated like Lumentum (think: Innolight/eoptolink, and maybe others).

So these are active developments, just not public material yet and could be a new press release anytime.

- for CPO scale out which happens H2 2026 onward like $POET, $SIVE is a laser supplier for those players.

- scale up CPO is h2 2027, from Ayar and Nvidia’a NVLink CPO ecosystem and that’s the main volume ramp across optical players.

But this is where $SIVE looks like they mog every other player from supply chain mapping.

Since they’ve likely been working + designed in with $MRVL Celestial, Lightmatter, Lightelligence and the others since they were small way back when.

On top of things like this:

For foundries like $GFS, $SIVE is the reference laser.

Not even including massive companies like O-Net building ELS with $SIVE, likely for the Asian hyperscalers supply chains.

This is why $SIVE is by far my favorite laser chokepoint long.

Feels like they’re everywhere in new optical architectures starting beginning of 2027.

analysis.summary: 该推文看好Sivers（$SIVE）在下一代光学架构中的核心地位，认为其不仅是CPO扩展的激光器供应商，还已与Jabil开发1.6T光收发器，并与其他插拔式厂商合作。预计从2027年起，Sivers将成为多个光学架构的关键瓶颈，具有长期投资价值。
analysis.translation: 这是一个误解：$SIVE不仅是CPO规模扩展的激光器供应商，更是下一代架构的激光器供应商。包括可插拔、CPO规模扩展、CPO规模升级、NPO等。Sivers和$JBL已经进入“神级模式”，开发了采用连续波激光器的1.6T光收发器，有效绕过了EML瓶颈，甚至提高了能效。根据Jabil管理层的说法，他们创造了“相对显著的护城河”。因此，对于下一代1.6T可插拔收发器，Sivers似乎立即被采用。市场也忽略了这一点：Jabil公告后，其他可插拔厂商已联系Sivers，目前正在合作（可能处于共同开发、认证阶段）。制造光收发器的厂商不多，且不像Lumentum那样垂直整合（例如Innolight/eoptolink等）。这些都是正在进行的开发，只是尚未公开，可能随时有新的新闻稿。对于CPO规模扩展（2026年下半年开始），如$POET，$SIVE是这些玩家的激光器供应商。CPO规模升级在2027年下半年，来自Ayar和Nvidia的NVLink CPO生态系统，这是光学玩家的主要量增。但在这里，$SIVE似乎从供应链映射上领先其他所有玩家，因为他们很可能早在与$MRVL Celestial、Lightmatter、Lightelligence等合作时就已参与设计。此外，对于$GFS等代工厂，$SIVE是参考激光器。甚至不包括O-Net等大型公司正在与$SIVE共建ELS，可能面向亚洲超大规模数据中心供应链。这就是为什么$SIVE是我最看好的激光瓶颈长期投资标的。感觉从2027年初开始，他们在新的光学架构中无处不在。

content:
I've been getting a lot of questions about OE Solutions (138080) recently. 

Here's my research/thoughts so far on it: 

They're a small Korean optical transceiver company, similar to $AAOI. 

And they've become one the few EML players in the world (eg. $COHR, $LITE, Mitsubishi, Source, Sumitomo), with scarce 100G EML laser capacity for 800G/1.6T. 

OE appears to be trying to make the full transceiver, not just the EML. They also have finished ELSFP CPO products, with UHP  CW lasers, which is sampling Q3. 

Likewise, OE also seems to be building out the full ELSFP, rather than selling CW laser dies, so that's more market share. 

So you can think of it as Korean AAOI but EML instead of CW, and less capacity/qualifications. And playing catch-up to the rest of the world. 

However in terms of timelines:

1. 23dBm cooled ELSFP samples start in Q3 2026
2. Sales base for 800G, and 2027 1.6T "full force"
3. Late H2 2027, H1 2028 onward probably their ELSFP enters the volume production. 

And it seems they're working on 200G EML capability from their investor snipper 2025 OE IR snippet referenced “100GBaud EML / 200G PAM". This seems promising given their IP/demos.

ELS has just been unveiled recently, sampling starts Q3. 800G/1.6T Optical transceivers are also likely 2027.

So this is basically Korea's sovereign photonics player, playing catchup to $LITE, $AAOI, and the bigger players.

(Disclosure: I have positions in OE Solutions (138080). This is fundamental research for informational purposes, not financial advice).

As for OE solution valuations: 

1.  Doesn't seem like there's confirmed customers yet for these growth verticals. 
2. Probably not many people understood what they're building toward yet. 
3. Yields data kinda uncertain

1. Customers: We're actually in a major EML/CW laser shortage, so any independent capacity will be sought after. I personally don't think they'll have a hard time finding customers here. I would assume anything they make might get bought out and would get extra support from Korea. 

2. Institutional support: Probably not much since it doesn't meet threshold for many US institution given MC size + KR listing. I also don't think many people understood what they're building yet. 

3. Yields/Capacity: OE's disclosed wafer/module utilization is low (i remember was around 31% underutilized off the top of my head), so there's enough material revenue they can generate before they need to spend on capex. 

I'm also not sure about EML/CW and other yields. Probably need to go ask the company. 

Is this some random crap co? No. It's been doing optical transceivers stuff for more than 20Y, has R&D in the U.S. and Netherlands. 

But their entire AI growth vertical seems to happen next year, and hinges mainly around capacity/yields.

And I personally think the EML/CW tech is probably worth a lot more than their current MC, if it were an acquisition target. 

Especially from  a larger player that wanted to vertically integrate EML for pluggables and CW lasers for CPO. 

Markets are probably waiting on more certainty around qualifications after Q3 sampling or earnings projections announcements.  

TLDR on thoughts:

Some of my friends discussed this last year, was probably way too early. Saw it got many comments 2 months ago, still too early. 

I still think now is early, but later in Q3-Q4 might be more interesting. I personally think it's a lot higher risk than a major CPO player like $SIVE, that's embedded in Ayar, $JBL, $GFS, and many other hyperscaler suppliers. Which also has Win Semi and others de-risking volume ramp. 

As OE Solutions looks like a new player trying to build out an $AAOI for the optical transceivers but sovereign EML and CW laser production for CPO products. 

And there's a lot of answered questions around customers + volume ramp, which presents material risk. 

But if you believe Korea can build out an 800G/1.6T transceiver EML supply chain and ELSFP with CW lasers. 

With OE Solutions, it might be worth taking a look into. Still researching the company tho, just initial thoughts.

analysis.summary: 该推文分析了韩国光收发器公司OE Solutions，认为其拥有稀缺的100G EML激光器产能，有望受益于800G/1.6T光模块需求增长，但短期风险较高，客户/产能不确定性大，建议关注Q3-Q4的催化剂。作者对其长期技术价值持乐观态度。
analysis.translation: 我最近收到很多关于OE Solutions（138080）的问题。以下是我目前的研究/想法：这是一家韩国小型光收发器公司，类似$AAOI。它已成为全球少数EML厂商之一（如$COHR、$LITE、三菱、源杰、住友），拥有稀缺的800G/1.6T所需100G EML激光器产能。OE似乎正试图制造完整的收发器，而不仅仅是EML。他们也有完成ELSFP CPO产品，配备UHP CW激光器，将于Q3送样。同样，OE似乎也在构建完整的ELSFP，而不是只卖CW激光器晶圆，所以市场份额更大。你可以把它看作是韩国版的AAOI，只不过用EML替代CW，产能和认证较少，正在追赶世界其他厂商。时间线上：1. 23dBm冷却ELSFP样品于2026年Q3开始送样；2. 2027年800G和1.6T“全面发力”；3. 2027年下半年至2028年上半年ELSFP可能进入量产。他们还在研发200G EML能力。这基本上是韩国的自主光子学玩家，正在追赶$LITE、$AAOI等大厂。（披露：我持有OE Solutions头寸。这是仅供参考的基础研究，不构成投资建议。）关于估值：客户尚未确认、机构支持有限、良率数据不确定。但我认为EML/CW技术可能比当前市值有价值得多，如果是收购目标的话。市场可能在等待Q3送样后的认证或盈利预期。TLDR：现在可能还早，但Q3-Q4可能更有趣。个人认为风险高于SIVE等主要CPO玩家。如果你相信韩国能构建800G/1.6T收发器EML供应链，OE Solutions值得关注。仍在研究，仅为初步想法。

analysis.translation:
现在海力士276万韩元，距离390万韩元重估市值还有50%空间，这一段市场重估的钱会在45天内给捏住海力士的人。美股adr上线之日就是重估完成之时，机构不会给散户太多利润空间，就跟他们操作新股ipo一样，吃到极致的估值再卖给散户。

AI不需要折腾太多瞎搞，只需要🤏捏住AI的蛋蛋。
我现在的仓位一半是海里，另一半是2x海力士，只有1%现金仓位。
如果算上机动资金则是我预留了额外25%的钱在市场发生动荡的情况下心态能平和游刃应对，经历过今年三月黑天鹅，我改变了以往完全彻底孤注一掷，现在始终给自己一个发生暴跌情况下抄底的机会。

content:
Trump administration is prob realizing now that the key to winning Trade Wars:

Is through frontier supply chains like Quantum, AI, Robotics.

Not cheap Nike sock exports.

With $ASML, $TOWA, $LPK, Ajinomoto,  
over in Japan, EU, TW, Korea, and others holding all the cards…

Since each country holds different monopolies and chokepoints needed to make each industry work.

Which all happens to be OUTSIDE the US.

Tariffing all our partners turned out to be not a great idea. 

But not too late imo to repair relations and weaponize global supply chains through partners if Trump wants better trade deals.

analysis.summary: 推文认为美国在贸易战中依赖盟友的尖端供应链（如量子计算、AI、机器人）而非低端出口，且关键垄断在境外，关税策略可能效果不佳。建议修复关系并利用全球供应链争取更好协议。
analysis.translation: 特朗普政府现在可能意识到，赢得贸易战的关键在于前沿供应链，如量子计算、AI、机器人，而不是廉价的耐克袜子出口。ASML、TOWA、LPK、味之素等公司分别在日本、欧盟、台湾、韩国等地持有制造各行业所需的不同垄断和瓶颈环节，而这些均不在美国境内。对合作伙伴加征关税结果并不理想。不过，如果特朗普想达成更好的贸易协议，现在修复关系并利用全球供应链作为武器还为时不晚。

content:
In terms of personal notes:

1. Priortech owns 21% of $CAMT, ~1.35x their MC, seems more like holding co.

2. Bit Digital people kept shouting, but NAV discount is not clean $WYFI, lot of dilution + not AI parent. 

3. Wistron (3231) is the best one I've seen since $SIVE + Ayar + Wiwynn are one of my favorite trio. 

~$16.2B MC, Q1 revenue up a ridiculous amount with 144% Y/Y growth. 

Owns 35.46% of Wiwynn, which is ~0.66x of MC. I'd expect Wiwynn to keep on growing. 

4. Sin-American Silicon 5483 does own 46.64% of GlobalWafers (6488). 

MC is $3.5B, stake value is ~$7.9B, but not exactly growing too fast independently, though it's heavily trading heavily discount to NAV. 

5. Iljin Holdings owns 42.99% of Iljin Electric, it's ~$0.22B MC vs. ~$1.13B NAV holding. This was transformer/cable DC related. 

But again idk if I trust korean stocks for NAV unlock, probably better for activist investors. 

6. Simmtech Holdings was ~$0.17B MC vs. ~$1.0B of parent, which also looks ridiculous. 

So 6x+ NAV. PCB substrate related name. Also another prob don't trust korean governance type name. 

I think both $ACMR and WUS have H-Share subsidiary listing soon, and those were the biggest NAV discounts with independent growth. 

Then you have a highly successful activist going after WUS so I'd expect some middle ground there. 

So maybe I'll put more concentration into those on top of what I already own Monday, we'll see. And Wistron was growing very fast independently. 

I'm still doing research, normally this goes in shower thoughts since I haven't formally made a conclusion, but thought others might be interested.

analysis.summary: 作者分析了多个折价交易机会，特别是Wistron（3231）增长强劲，并看好ACMR和WUS的H股上市及股东积极行动，倾向于集中投资这些标的。
analysis.translation: 个人笔记：1. Priortech持有CAMT 21%股权，市值约1.35倍，更像控股公司。2. Bit Digital的人一直喊，但NAV折价不清，WYFI稀释多，不是AI母公司。3. Wistron (3231)是我见过最好的，SIVE + Ayar + Wiwynn是我最喜欢的三组合。市值约162亿美元，Q1营收同比增144%。持有Wiwynn 35.46%股权，约市值0.66倍，预计Wiwynn继续增长。4. Sin-American Silicon 5483持有GlobalWafers (6488) 46.64%股权。市值35亿美元，持股市值约79亿美元，但独立增长不快，不过交易折价大。5. Iljin Holdings持有Iljin Electric 42.99%股权，市值2.2亿美元 vs 11.3亿美元NAV，变压器电缆DC相关。但不信任韩国股解锁，更适合积极投资者。6. Simmtech Holdings市值1.7亿美元 vs 母公司10亿美元，6倍以上NAV，PCB基板相关，同样不信任韩国治理。我认为ACMR和WUS都有H股子公司即将上市，是最大的NAV折价且有独立增长的。加上WUS有成功积极投资者介入，期望折价收窄。所以可能在我已持有的基础上再集中这些仓位，周一看。Wistron独立增长很快。仍在研究，通常这是淋浴时的想法，尚未正式结论，但觉得他人可能感兴趣。

content:
RT @TheBronxViking: $MU $400 calls that were alerted March 31st expired today and were hands down a top five, if not the single most unusual trade I have ever seen.🏆

The size that came in behind this never scaled out. Held start to finish in full.

3/31/2026, it hit the tape. 30,584 contracts at the ask and mid, avg fill 18.65. 
Total premium laid out around $57M. 
No meaningful selling after that, held the entire time.

Today at expiration those same contracts are worth $719.59 each. 
Paid $1,865, worth $71,959 now. 
That’s a +3,758% return.🤯🤯🤯🤯

Position value around $2.2B. 
Total gain around $2.14B.

Three months, March to June, OVER $2 BILLION in profit on one single position. 💰

If you tailed this trade you’re up +3,758%. Hands down one of the biggest examples ever of how it can literally pay to follow the flow. @unusual_whales 🐳

总投入约5700万美元。

支付1865美元，现在价值71,959美元。
收益率+3,758%。🤯🤯🤯🤯

头寸价值约22亿美元。
总获利约21.4亿美元。

三个月内，从三月到六月，单笔交易盈利超过20亿美元。💰

content:
$LPK 

Meeting Notes (not meant to be official guidance just a summary of what I heard)   

It's incredibly bullish imo a lot of great info but I'll leave that to the reader to decide. Most salient points are in bold 

1. Industry is ready for first high volume ramps 

2. Sees a minimum of 3 players initiating purchases (4 or 5 base case) this year with around 3-10 machine orders each... 

3. Previous delays are not down to lide as it was production ready but down to independent metalization which was immature... this is now more or less solved  

4. Last year the internal assessment was an addressable market of 500 million euros... this has changed and will greatly exceed this. Company intends to update this model soon  

5. Customers are now showing more willingness to take on risk 

6. CEO's target is 70% market share  

7. Share of glass in total package technologies in 2030 could be anywhere from 30% on the consrvative side to 80% on the more optimistic side. (this is not market share btw) 

8. Rev per system is something like 1 million euros... for initial ramp up 3-10 mill per buyer depending on how many systems are purchased

9. 100 million machine orders from a single customer in the future is something they stated they have to be ready for (100 mill rev on a single order!) Scaling strategy... 

10. Solar rev down but very strong potential once perovskite cells enter high volume stage (technological transition) 

11. Not surprised by Price action and believes it's reasonably priced 

12. Advanced packaging rev will be significantly greater than what other segments deliver in th 

13. LIDE is only the entry ticket, there are many other adjacent rev opportunities in advanced packaging 

14. Listing on a high volume exchange (like NASDAQ) is actively on their radar and discussions

analysis.summary: 该推文详细分析了LPK公司的投资前景，包括行业即将迎来高容量生产、至少3-5个买家今年将下单、金属化问题已解决、市场预期远超5亿欧元、客户风险偏好上升、CEO目标70%市场份额、先进封装收入将大幅增长、以及考虑在纳斯达克上市等利好消息。
analysis.translation:
$LPK

会议纪要（非官方指引，仅为我所听到的摘要）

在我看来非常看好，很多好信息，但我让读者自行判断。最要点已加粗。

1. 行业已准备好进行首次高容量生产。
2. 预计今年至少有3家买家启动采购（基准情况4或5家），每家订购3至10台机器。
3. 之前的延迟并非由于LIDE技术，因为它已可量产，而是由于独立的金属化工艺不成熟……现在这个问题基本解决。
4. 去年内部评估的可达市场为5亿欧元……现在这一数字将大幅超过。公司计划很快更新模型。
5. 客户现在更愿意承担风险。
6. CEO的目标是70%的市场份额。
7. 玻璃在总封装技术中的份额到2030年可能在保守的30%到乐观的80%之间（这不是市场份额）。
8. 每套系统的收入约为100万欧元……对于初期出货，每个买家收入在300万至1000万欧元之间，取决于购买的机器数量。
9. 未来来自单一客户的1亿欧元机器订单是他们必须准备面对的（单笔订单1亿收入！）规模化策略……
10. 太阳能收入下降，但一旦钙钛矿电池进入高容量生产阶段（技术转型），潜力巨大。
11. 对价格走势不感意外，认为其定价合理。
12. 先进封装收入将显著超过其他业务板块。
13. LIDE只是入场券，先进封装领域还有许多其他周边收入机会。
14. 在高容量交易所（如纳斯达克）上市正在积极考虑和讨论中。

Just a reminder: 99% of X was bearish on memory 3 months ago...

Since then:

$MU $380 -> $1122 (+195.26%)
$SNDK $565 -> $2155 (+281.42%)
$EWY $132 -> $219 (+65.91%)
SK Hynix 849K -> 2.685M KRW (+215.5%)
Samsung 172K -> 362K KRW (+110.7%)

If you see a bunch of projections on memory names like Samsung becoming the most profitable company in the world in 2028. 

Might be a good idea to think independently outside of the narratives at the time like "Oil, LNG, Helium, Iran, etc."

Probably same thing now with optical names and their projections into 2027, 2028.  

I do think photonics and memory are the 2 top themes though, with optics being very early into the supercycle.

analysis.summary: 该推文强调独立思考、避免跟风，看好存储器（如三星、SK海力士）和光子学（光学）的长期超级周期机会，认为当前市场对光学股的悲观预期类似三个月前对存储器的误判。
analysis.translation: 提醒一下：三个月前，99%的X（用户）对存储器持悲观态度...自那时起：MU $380 -> $1122 (+195.26%)；SNDK $565 -> $2155 (+281.42%)；EWY $132 -> $219 (+65.91%)；SK海力士 849K -> 2.685M韩元 (+215.5%)；三星 172K -> 362K韩元 (+110.7%)。如果你看到一堆关于存储器名称（如三星成为2028年全球最赚钱公司）的预测。也许最好独立思考，跳出当时流行的“石油、液化天然气、氦气、伊朗等”叙事。现在光学名称及其对2027、2028年的预测可能也是如此。不过，我确实认为光子学和存储器是两大主题，而光学则处于超级周期的早期阶段。

content:
存储这波不仅卡住了AI的脖子，
也卡了传统电子产品的脖子。
存储涨完什么涨？
当然还是存储涨！
现在的存储不仅赚走了AI绝大部分的利润，
还赚走了所有科技电子产品的利润。
存储是正儿八经王者中的王者行业，
海力士则是其中存储中的王者。
捏死就对了，
现在消费端的需求到了不得不买的时候。
不采购，那么传统电子产品就关门，
采购，那就继续捏着鼻子高价买存储商的存储造自己的电子产品。
采购不到货的消费电子则会直接倒闭失去市场，
亏损采购的现金流不够给存储商上贡的也会倒闭。

analysis.summary: 该推文强烈看好存储行业，认为存储是王者中的王者，海力士是存储中的王者，并指出当前消费端需求迫使厂商不得不高价采购，存储商将赚取绝大部分利润。
analysis.translation: 存储这波不仅卡住了AI的脖子，也卡了传统电子产品的脖子。存储涨完什么涨？当然还是存储涨！现在的存储不仅赚走了AI绝大部分的利润，还赚走了所有科技电子产品的利润。存储是正儿八经王者中的王者行业，海力士则是其中存储中的王者。捏死就对了，现在消费端的需求到了不得不买的时候。不采购，那么传统电子产品就关门，采购，那就继续捏着鼻子高价买存储商的存储造自己的电子产品。采购不到货的消费电子则会直接倒闭失去市场，亏损采购的现金流不够给存储商上贡的也会倒闭。

content:
非常感谢中文社区对我最看好股票的投资思路进行的深度剖析！

总的来说，像 $AAOI 和 $SIVE 这样的激光公司是我个人的最爱，因为它们在拓展营收方面有着极大的想象空间。它们可以不仅仅局限于销售激光器，还能制造完整的光模块 以及各种光引擎或 ELS (外部光源) 组件。

又或者像 $COHR 那样，甚至可以向上游延伸进军衬底制造领域，从而实现垂直整合，并不断提升自身的毛利率和总潜在市场规模 TAM

这其中很大一部分的关键在于要率先精准踩中投资主线，毕竟去年很多人甚至都不觉得“光子学（photonics）”能成为一个真正的热门概念。

而且我认为最会让大多数人感到惊讶的是，我们目前仍处于极其早期的阶段。绝大多数的营收放量（爬坡）要到明年，也就是 2027 年的上半年乃至下半年才会真正开始！

不过，能看到中文社区大家持续不断的支持，真的让我感觉非常棒。我会尽我所能，不辜负大家给我起的 “白毛股神” 这个称号。

analysis.summary: 作者看好激光及光子学领域的股票，认为这些公司在光模块、光引擎和衬底制造等方面有成长空间，营收放量将在2027年才开始，当前处于早期阶段。
analysis.translation: 非常感谢中文社区对我最看好股票的投资思路进行的深度剖析！总的来说，像 $AAOI 和 $SIVE 这样的激光公司是我个人的最爱，因为它们在拓展营收方面有着极大的想象空间。它们可以不仅仅局限于销售激光器，还能制造完整的光模块以及各种光引擎或 ELS (外部光源) 组件。又或者像 $COHR 那样，甚至可以向上游延伸进军衬底制造领域，从而实现垂直整合，并不断提升自身的毛利率和总潜在市场规模 TAM。这其中很大一部分的关键在于要率先精准踩中投资主线，毕竟去年很多人甚至都不觉得“光子学（photonics）”能成为一个真正的热门概念。而且我认为最会让大多数人感到惊讶的是，我们目前仍处于极其早期的阶段。绝大多数的营收放量（爬坡）要到明年，也就是 2027 年的上半年乃至下半年才会真正开始！不过，能看到中文社区大家持续不断的支持，真的让我感觉非常棒。我会尽我所能，不辜负大家给我起的 “白毛股神” 这个称号。

content:
RT @AnthonySandford: We had a very interesting trade pointed out to @NicholasFNS and myself on the @unusual_whales Whale Watch stream today by one of the @unusual_whales community members, @RhymesMarket, on the $QURE 6/18 45c. This was the definition of "someone always knows." Let's take a dive into the trade.

Let's start by looking at the historical volume/OI tab. This is where everything starts. We can see back on 6/5, we had 100 contracts transacted at the bid. 100% of that volume turned into open interest on 6/8. On 6/8, we had 402 contracts traded at the bid, with 100% of that volume carrying over into 6/9. We had 3 sessions of no major transactions until we got to 6/15, where we saw another 1,103 contracts transacted, but this time all at the ask. All but 3 contracts carried over into 6/16. Prior to today, you can see on the net premium that this is a name that doesn't get a lot of attention, but we can see some major spikes in the net call premium over the last month.

On 6/17, we had news that the FDA moved AMT-130 from a delayed/high-risk path back toward a near-term accelerated approval filing, and $QURE finished the day up 78.44%!!!!!!!!!!!

With all the days combined, the rough average between all the contracts would be .27. With these contracts hitting a high of 5.03 today, this trader would have saw gains of 1,762.96% on this trade.

With a rough premium of $59,390, this trader turned that into over 1.07 million in profits.

Assuming this is the same trader, which we can't since these all came in at different times, but with the way the 100% of the volume carried over for multiple days, we can assume this is more than likely the same trader. We're seeing a ton of volume today, so it will be impossible to track this flow from here.

analysis.summary: 该推文分析了$QURE期权交易活动，指出在FDA批准AMT-130加速审批前，有大量买单提前布局，最终该股单日暴涨78.44%，交易者获利超100万美元。
analysis.translation:
转发 @AnthonySandford：我们与 @NicholasFNS 在 @unusual_whales 的 Whale Watch 直播中，由社区成员 @RhymesMarket 指出了一笔非常有趣的交易，涉及 $QURE 6/18 45c 期权。这正是“总有人先知道”的典型案例。让我们深入分析这笔交易。

首先查看历史成交量/未平仓合约（OI）标签。这里是一切的关键。6月5日，有100张期权以买价成交，100%的成交量在6月8日转为未平仓合约。6月8日，有402张期权以买价成交，100%的成交量延续至6月9日。随后三个交易日没有大额交易，直到6月15日，又有1,103张期权成交，但这次全部以卖价成交，除3张外全部延续至6月16日。在此之前，从净权利金可看出该标的很少受关注，但过去一个月净看涨期权权利金出现明显飙升。

6月17日，有消息称FDA将AMT-130从延迟/高风险路径转回至加速审批路径，$QURE当日收涨78.44%！！

结合所有交易日，这些期权的平均成本约为0.27美元。今日这些期权最高触及5.03美元，该交易者浮盈高达1,762.96%。

按约59,390美元的总权利金计算，该交易者将其变成了超过107万美元的利润。

假设这是同一交易者（虽然无法确定，因为交易在不同时间发生），但考虑到连续数日100%的成交量转为未平仓合约，基本可以判定是同一操盘者。今日成交量巨大，此后难以追踪该资金流。

The 1st large scale X study on stock market net worth has finished! 

From the 6000+ responses of those active on X finance, here's the results:

5.8% - $10M+
15% - $1m - $10M
35.9% - $100k - $1M
43.3% - under $100k

This is probably more representative of active followers.

Where 20.8 out of every 100 people have over $1,000,000+.

And 5.8 out of 100 have over $10,000,000. 

And 43.3% of the population are probably happier in life than the rest. 

Was just curious, fascinating figures. 

Definitely not representative of the total population, but just those active on Finance X.

analysis.summary: 该推文分享了一项针对活跃于金融X平台用户的净资产调查结果，显示20.8%的用户净资产超过100万美元，5.8%超过1000万美元。虽然数据不具普遍代表性，但反映了特定投资社群的高净值比例，为观察市场参与者财富分布提供了侧面参考。
analysis.translation: 首个关于股市净资产的大规模X研究已完成！基于6000多份活跃在X财经领域的回复，结果如下：5.8%的人净资产超过1000万美元，15%在100万美元到1000万美元之间，35.9%在10万到100万美元之间，43.3%低于10万美元。这可能更能代表活跃粉丝的情况。即每100人中有20.8人拥有超过100万美元的净资产，每100人中有5.8人超过1000万美元。而43.3%的人可能比其他人生活得更幸福。只是好奇，数据很吸引人。显然不代表整体人口，仅代表活跃在金融X平台的人。

content:
I agree with Palliser Capital here.

As a shareholder, it’s just so stupid how WUS (2316 TW) MC can be ~$1B MC.

While they own a 11.26% stake of WUS Kunshan, a $39.3B (¥282B CNY) company…

Which is worth $4.42B pre-tax. Just sell 1/4th or something?

And you get an extra ~$1.1B for PCB growth at the inflection point with AI demand.

Which is more than your current MC pre-tax, purely in cash. 

And there’s still $3.3B in assets + markets might revalue your company because of willingness to trim locked up assets.

Nobody is asking you to trim everything, but the NAV disconnect is insanity. 

WUS TW management should really reconsider.

analysis.summary: 该推文指出WUS（2316 TW）市值约10亿美元，但其持有的WUS昆山11.26%股份价值约44.2亿美元（税前），加上PCB业务在AI需求拐点处的增长潜力，导致NAV严重折价。建议管理层出售部分资产以释放价值。
analysis.translation: 我同意Palliser Capital的观点。作为股东，WUS（2316 TW）市值仅约10亿美元太愚蠢了。他们持有WUS昆山11.26%的股份，而WUS昆山是一家市值393亿美元（2820亿元人民币）的公司……这股权税前价值44.2亿美元。卖掉四分之一或什么的？然后你还能获得PCB业务在AI需求拐点处增长的约11亿美元额外价值。这比你们当前市值税前还要高，完全是现金。还有33亿美元的资产，再加上市场可能因你们愿意处置锁定资产而重新估值。没人要求你们卖掉所有，但NAV价差简直疯狂。WUS TW管理层真该重新考虑。

content: BREAKING: The US has reportedly released the full 14-point memorandum with Iran, outlining an immediate ceasefire, safe commercial passage through the Strait of Hormuz, phased sanctions relief, access to frozen Iranian assets, a $300 billion reconstruction framework, limits on Iran's nuclear program under IAEA oversight, and a 60-day timeline to negotiate a final agreement backed by a binding UN Security Council resolution, per Reuters

content:
市值上万亿的公司不管在哪个市场，就算再利空都不会跌太多的，他们都是要做各个指数的核心持仓的，不会有太大波动。
所以别看说 $spcx  市值太大了太虚就觉得可能跌特别多，其实不然，市值不仅仅代表市值，也代表着一个公司的融资能力和信用额度。
它市值到这个位置，会有源源不断的钱流向他，他也能从市场源源不断的拿到钱。
其他万亿公司也一样，不要瞎恐慌，
涨了看涨跌了看跌。
另外，#sk海力士 也是一样，现在它是韩国指数和全韩国人的希望，韩国人端午节不得集体凑钱梭哈海力士。接下来的45天是海力士绝对的强势期。

analysis.summary: 作者认为市值上万亿的公司即使有利空也不会大跌，因为这些公司是指数核心持仓，有强大的融资能力和信用，资金会持续流入。同时看涨SK海力士，认为其是韩国指数和国民的希望，接下来45天是强势期。
analysis.translation: 市值上万亿的公司不管在哪个市场，就算再利空都不会跌太多的，他们都是要做各个指数的核心持仓的，不会有太大波动。所以别看说 $spcx  市值太大了太虚就觉得可能跌特别多，其实不然，市值不仅仅代表市值，也代表着一个公司的融资能力和信用额度。它市值到这个位置，会有源源不断的钱流向他，他也能从市场源源不断的拿到钱。其他万亿公司也一样，不要瞎恐慌，涨了看涨跌了看跌。另外，#sk海力士 也是一样，现在它是韩国指数和全韩国人的希望，韩国人端午节不得集体凑钱梭哈海力士。接下来的45天是海力士绝对的强势期。

content:
Don’t think there’s any new news from $XFAB. 

Their company needs to do a better job with marketing materials though.

Since nobody thinks they’re optical ai dc or power semi adjacent exposure apparently.

However, I would have thought that since they’re leading Europe’s silicon photonics value chain efforts with PhotonixFab.

And apparently everyone + EU are discussing sovereign AI supply chains after Fable’s export controls.

One might think they’re a major beneficiary alongside IMEC, ligentec and others in the sovereign AI push?

Anyway, markets can always disagree or maybe they didn’t link things together.

Regardless, I’m just curious how this plays out.  (Disclosure, own positions)

analysis.summary: 作者认为XFAB作为欧洲硅光子学价值链的领导者，可能成为欧盟主权AI供应链政策的主要受益者，但目前市场尚未充分认识其价值，持仓观望。

content:
Trendforce reports that $AMD is actively trying to secure CW laser supply with multiple major procurement orders...

Is probably just the start of the bottleneck?  

There's not much independent capacity in Western supply chains left other than $SIVE or $AAOI and maybe Macom. 

Especially after Lumentum/Coherent got locked up with multi-year agreements with Nvidia. (disclosure, own Sive and aaoi)

Lumentum is already CW laser constrained and is likely buying off Japanese companies like Sumitomo/Furukawa if I had to guess per ER, and those are probably running at max capacity.  

From the Trendforce report, this is:

"to ensure that its future capacity will not be constrained by NVIDIA and other major Cloud Service Providers (CSPs)."

I wouldn't be surprised if other hyperscalers like Amazon, Microsoft, and others saw Nvidia / AMD signing LTAs, and are trying to secure capacity next. 

A lot of it is game theory on not getting choked out by competitors, and looks like AMD is tipping the first domino after Nvidia.

But my opinion is that this just goes and show how invaluable this CW laser chokepoint is and the companies are inside it.

analysis.summary: 推文指出AMD积极下单锁定CW激光器供应，暗示该环节可能成为瓶颈。分析认为西方供应链中独立产能有限，相关公司如SIVE、AAOI和Macom将受益；并提及Lumentum和Coherent已与英伟达签订多年协议，导致资源紧张。整体看好CW激光器领域的投资价值。
analysis.translation: Trendforce报告称，AMD正积极通过多个大批量采购订单确保连续波（CW）激光器的供应...这可能只是瓶颈的开始？西方供应链中剩余独立产能的公司不多，除了SIVE、AAOI，或许还有Macom。尤其是在Lumentum/Coherent与英伟达签署多年协议之后。（披露：本人持有SIVE和AAOI）Lumentum的CW激光器已经受限，据财报推测，它很可能在从住友、古河等日本公司采购，而这些公司可能已满负荷运转。根据Trendforce报告，此举是“为确保其未来产能不受英伟达及其他主要云服务提供商（CSP）的限制”。我认为其他云巨头如亚马逊、微软也可能看到英伟达/AMD签署长期协议后，会尝试确保产能。这很大程度上是博弈论——避免被竞争对手排挤——看起来AMD是继英伟达之后推倒第一张多米诺骨牌的公司。但我的观点是，这进一步显示了CW激光器这一瓶颈的宝贵性，以及处于其中的公司价值。

content:
So I’m reading about an activist report about WUS (2316) right now from Palliser.

I actually thought their report was super compelling… that I decided to follow along and buy in as well. (not my idea this time, I own shares, had to change some weightings around to add this in).

Basically WUS Taiwan owns 11.3% of Kunshan (which is 45% Nvidia AI switch PCB share from their ests.).

WUS TW is valued ~$1B, probably from their own standalone PCB business, which does around $120M revenue, with AI growth ahead.

That stake of WUS Kunshan is worth around $4-4.3B MC.

After tax it’s around $3.2B, which by itself is 3-4x the current business value.

And they’re trying to pressure management to unlock that value.

I looked at WUS for PCB exposure earlier, and thought it was an interesting idea but didn’t know about ownership.

And this should derisk downside quite a bit. 

Main risk is that is management ignores them completely, but don’t think it will be completely ignored like Snapchat. 

Maybe some middle ground like selling 1/3rd stake?

Apparently it’s some family business that’s stubborn but feel like activists can do some work.

Anyway just doing research into this now sharing my findings.

analysis.summary: 推文分析WUS（2316）的投资价值，指出其持有WUS昆山11.3%股权，后者供应Nvidia AI开关PCB，价值被低估，且存在积极股东要求释放价值。作者已买入，认为下行风险有限。
analysis.translation: 我正在阅读Palliser关于WUS（2316）的积极股东报告。他们的论证非常有说服力，以至于我决定跟进并买入。（这次不是我的主意，我持有股票，不得不调整一些权重以加入这只股票）。基本上，WUS台湾持有昆山公司11.3%的股份（据估计，昆山占据了Nvidia AI交换机PCB约45%的份额）。WUS台湾的估值约为10亿美元，这可能来自于其独立的PCB业务，该业务收入约1.2亿美元，并且随着AI增长前景看好。WUS昆山那部分股权的市值大约在40-43亿美元。税后价值约为32亿美元，这本身就相当于当前业务价值的3-4倍。他们正在向管理层施压，要求释放这一价值。我之前关注过WUS的PCB业务，觉得这个想法有趣，但不知道其股权结构。而这一点应该能大幅降低下行风险。主要风险是管理层完全无视他们，但我认为不会像Snapchat那样被完全忽视。也许是个折中方案，比如出售1/3的股份？据说这是一个固执的家族企业，但我觉得积极股东可以有所作为。总之，我正在就此进行研究，并分享我的发现。

content:
I’m not sure why many folks are super bearish on my high conviction $AAOI long… 

Ever since $30, then on the way up to $170. (Yes I do think every bear is wrong, we’ll see who’s right).

They have scarce laser capacity that $AMD and other hyperscalers are looking for.

While the entire industry is bottlenecked by $NVDA. 

Along with a US transceiver supply chain for mass production of 800g/1.6T (management - largest in America).

While demand far exceeds supply and while assembly gets outsourced to Asia.

Then they’re quoting $471M monthly revenue in H1 entering H2 of 2027. Which is $5.6B ARR, off a $13.5B MC…

While a lot of major inflection volume hits even later in 2028.

As for fluctuations, there might be active $600M ATMs that get tapped into at random times. 

And random bear posts + macro from time to time that cause more volatility (eg. Analyst notes saying bear on $LITE due to false CPO delay rumors, then that brings down others in the sector).

Also we’re a year out so timelines are still a little early.

I haven’t seen such fast revenue ramp since $NBIS.

analysis.summary: 推文强烈看多AAOI，认为其激光产能稀缺、受益于800G/1.6T光模块需求，预计2027年H1月收入4.71亿美元，对应年化收入56亿美元，市值仅135亿美元，存在巨大上涨空间。同时提醒短期波动来自ATM增发和行业传言。
analysis.translation: 我不确定为什么很多人对我的高信心$AAOI多头仓位极度看空……从30美元开始，一路上涨到170美元。（是的，我认为每个空头都是错的，我们看看谁是对的）。他们拥有稀缺的激光产能，AMD和其他超大规模数据中心正在寻找这种产能。而整个行业都受限于$NVDA。再加上美国收发器供应链用于大规模生产800G/1.6T（管理层称是美国最大的）。在需求远超供应、组装外包到亚洲的情况下。他们给出的2027年H1进入H2的月收入是4.71亿美元，即年化56亿美元，而市值仅135亿美元……而更大的盈利拐点量在2028年晚些时候才会到来。至于波动，可能有6亿美元的ATM会在随机时间被使用。还有随机的空头帖子和大宏观事件时不时引发更多波动（例如，分析师看空$LITE的假CPO延迟传言，然后行业其他股票也跟着下跌）。另外我们还有一年时间，时间线仍有点早。我还没见过像$NBIS那样快速的营收增长。

content:
Mizuho Research: No delays on CPO or 800v dc. 

Revised up optical engine projections from $NVDA demand ramp.  

- Next phase in CPO for long term, believes InP DFB lasers remains the focus (hello $SIVE). VSCEL and microLED remain "unproven" in short distance in rack + chip to chip for 1.6T+ 

- HVDC deployment on track, 800VDC incremental volume 2027, with higher penetration in 2028.

What a stupid CPO related selloff recently.

analysis.summary: 瑞穗研究指出CPO和800V DC没有延迟，上调了NVDA驱动的光学引擎预测，认为InP DFB激光器是重点，HVDC部署如期，800VDC增量在2027-2028年，批评近期CPO相关回调不合理。整体看好相关技术及个股。
analysis.translation: 瑞穗研究：CPO或800V DC没有延迟。因英伟达需求上升，上调光学引擎预测。- CPO的下一阶段长期来看，InP DFB激光器仍是重点（你好 $SIVE）。VSCEL和microLED在1.6T+的短距离机架内和芯片到芯片方面仍“未经证实”。- HVDC部署按计划进行，800VDC增量在2027年，2028年渗透率更高。最近的CPO相关抛售真愚蠢。

content:
Just some random thoughts, I do think AI is the most disruptive technology in human history. 

To the level of agricultural or industrial revolution.

Since Anthropic, OpenAi, XAI, and others are racing to build superintelligence. 

The amount of economic impact can't be measured if AI helps find cures for cancer or accelerates discovery for Quantum Computing. 

Or if AI end up displacing the workforce, which increases profitability for companies. 

The US Gov has every incentive to keep the buildout going too, as the implications from Warfare, Cybersecurity, is also immeasurable if China takes the lead. 

So there's likely to be incentives and subsidies to win, even if there's not enough profit derived LLM training/inference. 

As for sustainability, when you look upstream, $GOOGL is able to fund it majorly with their own cashflow, same with $AMZN, $MSFT. 

More lukewarm on $META. Very iffy about $ORCL. 

But I do see some bubbles forming around debt interest like $CRWV.

Maybe circular valuations that's happening with OpenAI backlog agreements or $NVDA / $AMD agreements with Neoclouds to buy their GPUs.

But as seen with $MSFT and having OpenAI be a major part of the backlog, it did correct off the information, so "bubbles" like that do pop despite the overall markets increasing.

Definitely don't see a bubble in upstream semiconductors from $LITE to Sk Hynix though since the amount of profit they get from the buildout would likely be insane to make up for capex decreasing. 

OpenAI was actually my biggest fear from contagion, eg. $CRWV, $CBRS and others, but they just raised a lot.

So think it will be fine for another 1 1/2 years of capex, especially if they IPO this year. 

I also don't think we'll get massive Fed tightening despite "predictions" since this will trigger a contagion since many of these players rely heavily on debt. 

And although the Fed is independent, don't think Trump would have supported someone who is against his administration goals.  

As for semiconductor valuations going up every day like $AMD or $MU, there's probably going to be some corrections here and there. Everything going up together is kinda unhealthy. 

Can't time the capex peak but just from $AVGO and other projections, it just keeps accelerating exponentially into 2028. 

Especially as everyone is starting to sign multi year agreements as well. 

OpenAI contagion / hyperscaler capex decreasing / fed tightening was what I'm looking out for, and no blaring signs of any of those yet. 

So I think the music will keep playing for this year at the bare minimum.

analysis.summary: 分析认为AI是颠覆性技术，中美竞争下美国政府会持续补贴，上游半导体如NVDA、AMD盈利强劲，泡沫风险可控，未来1-2年资本支出持续增长。
analysis.translation: 只是一些随想，我认为AI是人类历史上最具颠覆性的技术，堪比农业或工业革命。Anthropic、OpenAI、XAI等公司正在竞相构建超级智能。如果AI帮助找到癌症治疗方法或加速量子计算发现，其经济影响难以估量。或者如果AI最终取代劳动力，提高公司盈利能力。美国政府也有充分激励推进建设，因为如果中国领先，战争和网络安全的影响同样不可估量。因此，即使LLM训练/推理利润不足，也可能有激励和补贴。关于可持续性，上游来看，谷歌、亚马逊、微软能用自己的现金流资助，我对Meta较冷淡，对Oracle非常不确定。但我看到一些泡沫围绕债务利息如CRWV。OpenAI的积压协议或NVDA/AMD与云商的GPU交易可能存在循环估值。但正如微软将OpenAI作为积压的一部分后价格修正，此类泡沫虽会破裂但市场整体仍在上升。上游半导体如LITE和SK海力士我不认为有泡沫，因为它们从建设中获得巨大利润。我原本最担心OpenAI的传染效应，如CRWV、CBRS等，但他们刚融了一大笔钱，所以未来1.5年资本支出没问题，尤其如果他们今年IPO。我也不认为会有大规模美联储紧缩，因为这会引发传染，许多玩家依赖债务。虽然美联储独立，但我不认为特朗普会支持反对其政府目标的人。半导体估值如AMD、MU每日上涨，可能会有调整。一切都同时上涨不太健康。无法预测资本支出峰值，但从博通等预测看，它呈指数增长到2028年，尤其大家开始签多年协议。我关注的OpenAI传染、超大规模资本支出减少、美联储紧缩，目前没有明显信号。所以我认为音乐至少今年还会继续。

content:
Yeah... I'm not quite sure why everyone likes just throwing personal accusations.

Like "scam" or  "memestock" every time I post a thesis. 

Like when I bought $AXTI (still holding shares btw):

- spent the time mapping the InP substrate supply chain
- found analyst sources around market share
- tracked high purity indium pricing on SMM
- modeled game theory around bottleneck hikes/supply shocks. 
- tracked Gov publications like seizure of optical companies or export controls 

Then just posted the idea for free, cause it's a hobby. 

Everyone just threw out personal insults as it went up in price...

Just for Reuters to confirm it 7M later that InP substrates can halt the AI buildout.

I'm just sharing ideas out for free, could always be wrong, and they usually take some time to play out. 

But would prefer instead if folks/media played devil's advocate on the thesis side like export controls or ASP pricing power...

Over just making up narratives or personal accusations like "memestock" or "foreign institutional team" when they lack the technical depth to understand AI supply chains.

analysis.summary: 作者认为AXTI是深度研究后的价值投资机会，并非迷因股，强调InP基板在AI建设中的关键作用，长期看好。
analysis.translation: 嗯...我不太明白为什么大家都喜欢进行人身攻击。比如我每次发布论文时，就会有人说“骗局”或“迷因股”。就像我买入$AXTI（至今仍持有）一样：我花时间梳理了InP基板供应链，从分析师那里找到了市场份额数据，跟踪了SMM上的高纯铟价格，建立了关于瓶颈涨价/供应冲击的博弈论模型，并查阅了政府文件（如光学公司扣押或出口管制）。然后我免费分享了这个想法，因为这只是个爱好。随着价格上涨，大家都开始人身攻击...直到7个月后，路透社证实InP基板可能会阻碍AI建设。我只是免费分享想法，我可能总是错的，而且这些想法通常需要时间才能实现。但我更希望人们/媒体能从论文角度进行魔鬼代言，比如出口管制或ASP定价能力...而不是编造叙事或人身攻击，比如“迷因股”或“外国机构团队”，因为他们缺乏技术深度来理解AI供应链。

That’s when I picked up a bunch to think that it goes from 11 to over 140 in a period of six months it’s insane. Sadly, I sold out between 60 and 70 and I don’t have this anymore.

content:
Fun throwback to random ideas back in 2025.

Back then, $AAOI was $2B MC, $LITE was a $26B MC, $AXTI was $500M

Now:
- AAOI is $15.37B
- Lumentum is $74.47B
- AXT is $7.24B

Was working off less information back then, given it was an early theme. 

Obviously nuances with ASIC programs might have been missed as more details came out. 

But directionally, glad thematically my ideas turned out correct.

content:
New reports that $AMD is scrambling for CW laser supply. 

And is negotiating large-scale purchase orders for CW Lasers to ensure its production capacity is not constrained by $NVDA (Trendforce)

Obvious CW laser beneficiaries: 
- $SIVE (AMD went to GFS for CPO, Sivers reference laser level)
- $AAOI (Rosenblatt analyst checks)

Lumentum/Coherent are kinda booked out way into 2028 as well. 

Lumentum is especially constrained for CW capacity already from existing EML contracts (so they probably are buying from Sumitomo/Furukawa and co). 

Maybe Macom and Japanese giants still have spare capacity. (disclosure, own aaoi/sivers).

I predicted this last year and said hyperscalers should go more upstream to secure capacity... at laser levels, epiwafer levels, or even inp substrate levels. 

To not get bottlenecked by Nvidia.

analysis.summary: 该推文指出AMD正在抢购连续波激光器产能以避免受英伟达制约，并点名受益股SIVE和AAOI，同时提到Lumentum/Coherent产能已排至2028年，暗示上游供应链存在投资机会。
analysis.translation: 新报告称$AMD正在抢购连续波激光器供应。并且正在协商大规模采购订单，以确保其生产能力不受$NVDA的限制（Trendforce）。明显的连续波激光器受益者：$SIVE（AMD转向GFS做CPO，Sivers参考激光器级别）、$AAOI（Rosenblatt分析师确认）。Lumentum/Coherent的产能也已排到2028年。Lumentum的连续波产能尤其紧张，因为已有EML合同（所以他们可能正在从住友/古河等公司采购）。也许Macom和日本巨头还有剩余产能。（披露：持有aaoi/sivers）。我去年就预测了这一点，并说超大规模企业应该更上游地锁定产能……在激光器级别、外延片级别甚至InP衬底级别。以免被英伟达卡脖子。

content:
Just as a recap, these were all my core European longs:

1. $SIVE
2. $LPK
3. $SOI
4. $RPI 
5. $IQE
6. $ALRIB
7. $XFAB

Sivers: As you know by now, core laser chokepoint over next generation photonics, from 1.6T pluggables to CPO.

Embedded in many hyperscaler suppliers from Jabil to Ayar. Should go brrr 2027 but markets are forward looking, so ramps + qualifications should get priced in now. 

LPK Laser - Glass core substrate "monopoly" with LIDE. 

"More than 80% of major global players have selected our equipment for process validation, learning and scaling to mass production"

Soitec - Silicon photonics SoI substrate pure monopoly while coming out of legacy drag segments.  

Raspberry Pi - Was my fun idea around Raspberry Pis being used for AI hardware deployments. 

Previously this thing was mainly educational or hobby boards, but now used for edge/local AI. Just thought revenue increase would be extremely material and it played out well. 

IQE - Critical epiwafer player for your Western photonics like Macom, Tower, Lumentum, and others. 

Was kinda going under, but thought their latent capacity relative to Landmark was undervalued. 

Also given how important it was, I thought that your downstream players + Govs wouldn't let it go under, so it was more of a moonshot idea earlier in the year. 

Lot more derisked now, very important.

Riber - Kinda monopoly in the MBE space, exposure to Quantum / quantum dot + silicon photonics. 

Found out from OSINT help from a friend latentvalue that Microsoft Quantum was buying their machines, so this was direct hyperscaler validation + kinda de-risked at current MCs.

XFab - SiC foundry backed by EU/US CHIPS Act with power semi upside. (152% Y/Y growth for their sic vertical). 

Main growth was their silicon photonics foundry past 2027 that's getting evaled by nvidia. And that they're leading Europe's value chain efforts in photonics, kinda like an early tower semi. 

We'll see how this plays out, thought power semi exposure + low P/B would derisk the company until they scale their photbunchonics efforts. 

From my own personal thoughts:

Out of the  maybe $SOI has already been re-rated the most? But I'm holding anyway. 

$LPK and $ALRIB I think are still undervalued despite their monopolies. 

$RPI is just kinda seeing how things go at this point, would be hilarious if they ended up like a mini nvidia for low end edge ai. 

$IQE probably has a long way to go given new tower long term agreement, alongside macom. And if they convert latent capacity, I still think it has a chance of rerating like landmark. 

$XFAB idk if im missing something or are markets missing something. you have nvidia as a direct eval of their silicon photonics foundry, and it's trading below replacement P/B. i think im right though. 

$SIVE I see has the highest upside out of all of them given laser company ability to vertically integrate, acquire companies downstream to make their lasers more valuable, etc. Just like coherent/lumentum. 

There's like 1-2 more random ones that aren't really material, but just in general.

These are the ones I've liked the most.

analysis.summary: 该推文详细分析了多只欧洲长期持有的股票，涵盖光电子、玻璃基板、硅光子、AI硬件等热门领域，作者对大部分标的持乐观态度，尤其看好SIVERS（激光芯片）、LPK（玻璃基板垄断）、ALRIB（MBE设备垄断）和XFab（SiC+硅光子代工）的上涨潜力。
analysis.translation: 以下是我核心的欧洲多头持仓：$SIVE、$LPK、$SOI、$RPI、$IQE、$ALRIB、$XFAB。SIVERS：下一代光子学的核心激光瓶颈，从1.6T可插拔到CPO，嵌入多家超大规模供应商（从Jabil到Ayar），2027年应会大爆发，但市场前瞻，现在就会定价爬坡和认证。LPK Laser：玻璃基板LIDE工艺“垄断”，超过80%的全球主要玩家已选择其设备进行工艺验证和量产爬坡。Soitec：硅光子SOI衬底纯垄断，同时摆脱传统拖累业务。Raspberry Pi：AI硬件部署的趣味想法，从教育/爱好者板转向边缘/本地AI，收入增长显著。IQE：西方光子学关键外延片供应商（服务Macom、Tower、Lumentum等），原本快倒闭，但认为其闲置产能被低估，且下游客户和政府不会让它倒闭，如今风险已大幅降低。Riber：MBE设备的垄断供应商，涉足量子点/硅光子，微软量子部门购买其机器，得到超大规模验证。XFab：SiC代工厂，受益于欧美芯片法案，功率半导体同比增长152%，主要增长点在于硅光子代工（2027年后），正在被英伟达评估，类似于早期的Tower Semiconductor。个人认为：SOI可能已重估最多，但仍持有；LPK和ALRIB仍然低估；RPI边走边看，若成为边缘AI的迷你英伟达会很有趣；IQE因新长期协议和Macom合作，仍有重估空间；XFab交易在净资产以下，而英伟达直接评估其硅光子代工，市场可能没看到这一点。SIVERS在垂直整合、并购下游公司等方面潜力最大。

content:
I’ve written a thesis on these 3 themes early on:

Neoclouds, Photonics, and Memory.

Now, it’s fun to sit back and watch all my thesis ideas play out from $AAOI to $EWY to $NBIS. 

Even got my warnings right too, $IREN is still stagnant due to the $6B of constant selling pressure from the ATM, while $NBIS reaches ATHs.

But the bagholders still don’t want to admit it.

Think a core part is knowing what theme comes next with markets, then comes picking the winner + heavy concentration in them.

If you went long on software and chose the ideal stock, you’d probably end up not as happy? 

Photonics is still probably the earliest out of the three.

But I can see Nebius end up like AWS one day.

And $MU / SK Hynix / Samsung potentially end up like a mini $NVDA if memory demand is structural.

analysis.summary: 作者早期布局了Neoclouds、光子学和内存三大主题，看好相关标的，如NBIS可能成长为类似AWS的公司，MU等内存股可能成为迷你版NVDA。
analysis.translation: 我早期就撰写了关于这三个主题的论文：新云、光子学和内存。现在，看着我的论文想法从$AAOI到$EWY到$NBIS逐步实现，真是有趣。我的警告也对了，$IREN由于ATM带来的60亿美元持续抛售压力而停滞不前，而$NBIS则创下历史新高。但那些持有者仍然不愿承认。我认为核心部分是知道市场接下来会流行什么主题，然后选择赢家并高度集中投资。如果你做多软件并选择了理想的股票，你可能最终不会那么开心？光子学可能仍是三者中最早的。但我可以看到Nebius有朝一日像AWS一样。而$MU / SK Hynix / 三星如果内存需求是结构性的，可能会成为迷你版的$NVDA。

content:
$SIVE is the next SIVE.

Don’t think you’ll find another company.

That’s qualified and likely primary/sole source with:

- $JBL and other pluggable hyperscaler suppliers 
- Ayar and the $NVDA NVLink CPO ecosystems

While being the foundational reference laser for $GFS and pluggable/CPO/NPO deployments.

That hyperscalers like $AMD and others use, at current valuations.

Even $POET buys $SIVE lasers and Poet is about the same valuation just off having one $50m purchase agreement. 

Amount of hyperscaler suppliers for 2027 into 2028 is just ridiculous. 

From the general meeting today in a few hours, we’ll hopefully see NASDAQ listing timelines confirmed.

So they can have room for M&A to TAM expansion and to make each laser they sell more valuable.

Following what $LITE did to grow into a $75B company.

analysis.summary: 该推文极度看好$SIVE，认为其是AI光互连核心供应商，拥有多家超大规模客户的认证和采购协议，估值低于$POET，并有望复制$LITE的增长路径，强调2027-2028年需求庞大，短期催化剂是纳斯达克上市时间表。
analysis.translation: $SIVE就是下一个SIVE。别以为你能找到另一家公司。这家公司已经获得资格认证，很可能成为独家/主供应商，服务于：- $JBL和其他可插拔超大规模供应商- Ayar和$NVDA NVLink CPO生态系统同时作为$GFS以及可插拔/CPO/NPO部署的基础参考激光器。那些超大规模企业如$AMD等都在用，按当前估值来看。就连$POET也购买$SIVE的激光器，而$POET的估值仅仅因为一笔5000万美元的采购协议就与$SIVE差不多。2027到2028年超大规模供应商的数量简直太夸张了。从今天几个小时后召开的股东大会，我们希望看到纳斯达克上市时间表的确认。这样他们就有空间进行并购以扩大TAM，并使他们销售的每颗激光器更有价值。效仿$LITE成长为750亿美元公司的道路。

content:
Today, there's a new report that China eased InP substrate exports. 

Which is expected to relieve mass production bottlenecks in the photonics market (source: Digitimes)

My optical positions are very happy to hear this:

From $AXTI (substrates), $IQE (epiwafers) to $AAOI (lasers) / $LITE / $SIVE, and others. 

Taiwanese optical players like VPEC, Landmark, and others should go brrr as well. 

Just to recap: the photonics market, especially for laser companies is moreso "how much can you make" rather than how much demand is there. 

InP substrates was one of the main bottlenecks affecting upstream capacity.   

So if you're able to make more = more revenue.

analysis.summary: 该推文提到中国放松InP衬底出口，缓解了光电子市场量产瓶颈，利好相关光学公司（如AXTI、IQE、AAOI等），并认为激光公司产能增加将直接推动收入增长。整体观点积极。
analysis.translation: 今天有新报道称中国放松了InP衬底的出口，预计这将缓解光电子市场的量产瓶颈（来源：Digitimes）。我的光学头寸对此非常高兴：从$AXTI（衬底）、$IQE（外延片）到$AAOI（激光器）/ $LITE / $SIVE等。台湾的光学公司如VPEC、Landmark等也应该会大涨。回顾一下：光电子市场，尤其是激光公司，更多是“你能生产多少”的问题，而不是需求有多大。InP衬底曾是限制上游产能的主要瓶颈之一。因此，如果你能生产更多 = 更多收入。

content:
This is gonna upset a lot of people: 

But TA is astrology for traders.

It's confirmation bias + trading human psychology about entries. 

Kinda like how people frontran $SPCE from $SPCX IPO expecting retail to mess up tickers by trading psychology. 

$SIVE didn't go up 1900% because of the golden cross space comet firebreathing dragon candle that someone is trying to sell for $499. 

It's because markets are pricing in future revenue from $JBL, $GFS that got announced. 

$AXTI didn't go up 8000% because the golden waterfall candle alert sounded back at $8. 

it's because of InP substrate, game theory on ASP hikes, export controls, photonics demand, and others. 

If you want to figure out psychologically what other regards are believing, you use TA. 

But for determining the actual upside... nah

People have been drawing $120+ TAs on $IREN for the past idk how many months none of that crap matters when there's a $6B ATM that needs to be bought through first.

It's by theme (eg. $LITE to $AAOI relations), any news catalysts that affect forward revenue, projections, macro news, earnings, float dynamics, and so on. 

Then you can just derive what MC that company should be at. 

So for entry points, sure you can use TA. 

For determining where the stock heads, just throw the tyrannosaurs rex omega-green candle indicator out the window.

analysis.summary: 推文强烈批判技术分析（TA），认为其类似占星术，充满确认偏差和交易心理学，而非基本面驱动。作者主张通过主题关联、新闻催化剂、宏观数据、收益和流通动态等基本面因素判断股价去向，仅将TA用于入场点的心理分析。文中列举了多个股票案例（如SIVE、AXTI、IREN），说明其涨幅源于未来收入预期、供需变化等实质因素，而非技术指标。整体对TA持否定态度，但未明确看多或看空市场。
analysis.translation: 这条推文会让很多人不爽：但技术分析（TA）对交易者来说就是占星术。它是关于入场点的确认偏差和交易心理学。有点像人们通过交易心理预期散户会搞错股票代码，从而在$SPCE从$SPCX IPO时就抢先交易一样。$SIVE没有因为某个卖499美元的‘黄金交叉太空彗星喷火龙蜡烛图’而上涨1900%。它上涨是因为市场正在定价来自$JBL和$GFS的未来收入，这些消息已经公布了。$AXTI没有因为‘黄金瀑布蜡烛预警’在8美元时响起而上涨8000%。它上涨是因为InP衬底、ASP上涨的博弈论、出口管制、光子需求等因素。如果你想在心理上弄清楚其他蠢货相信什么，你使用TA。但要确定实际上行空间……不。人们在$IREN上画$120以上的TA已经好几个月了，但那些东西都无关紧要，因为有一个60亿美元的ATM需要先被买入消化。它是根据主题（例如$LITE到$AAOI的关系）、任何影响未来收入的新闻催化剂、预测、宏观消息、收益、流通动态等等。然后你就可以得出那家公司应该有的市值。所以对于入场点，当然你可以使用TA。对于判断股价去向，就把那个霸王龙欧米伽绿色蜡烛指标扔出窗外吧。

content:
If I had to stereotype my X experiences with markets:

China 🇨🇳: set on cloning me with AI, can only think of trades in short term timeframes from A-shares PTSD.

America 🇺🇸:  bullish on anything futuristic like $SPCX, don’t care about valuations

Europe 🇪🇺: from $SIVE to $SOI, cares more about water usage than the AI buildout. Somehow can only look at past 12 months. (Belgium is cool so far), looking at you France + Sweden

Korea 🇰🇷: leveraged degens. I’ve never seen a market so volatile. Equivalent of 50x hyperliquid traders but with stock markets.

Japan 🇯🇵: somehow supportive of everything, haven’t seen any Japanese person aggressively bear post and short stocks before.

Not enough data on other places yet like Latin America, but will have some soon enough ig.

analysis.summary: 该推文通过刻板印象分析了不同国家投资者的行为特点：中国投资者受A股影响偏向短期交易；美国投资者追逐未来概念且不关注估值；欧洲投资者重视ESG（如用水量）且关注短期业绩；韩国市场高波动、杠杆率高；日本投资者普遍看多。无明确买卖建议。
analysis.translation:
如果让我对我在市场上（X平台）的经历进行刻板印象总结：

中国🇨🇳：打算用AI克隆我，只能从A股创伤后遗症出发以短期时间框架思考交易。

美国🇺🇸：看好任何像$SPCX这样的未来主义事物，不在乎估值。

欧洲🇪🇺：从$SIVE到$SOI，更关心用水量而不是AI建设。不知为何只能看过去12个月。（比利时目前还不错），特别是法国和瑞典。

韩国🇰🇷：杠杆赌徒。我从未见过如此波动的市场。相当于股票市场上的50倍杠杆Hyperliquid交易者。

日本🇯🇵：不知为何支持一切，没见过任何日本人激进地发布看空帖子和做空股票。

其他地区如拉丁美洲数据不足，但很快会有。

content:
한국 어머니들은 원래 이렇게 사기캐인가요?
어머님이 WF6의 3차 파급 효과(third-order effect)에 따른 공급망 차질을 파악하셨다니 정말 놀랍습니다.

중국의 대(對)일본 텅스텐 수출 통제로 인해,
전구체 부족 및 원가 상승이 발생하여 글로벌 공급망의 25~35%가 타격을 입게 된다는 사실을 말이죠.

게다가 후성(093370)이 SK하이닉스 같은 메모리 반도체 제조사들에게 얼마나 중요한 기업인지 짚어내시고,

이 공급망에 얽힌 수많은 기업들 중에서도 시가총액 12억 달러(약 1조 6천억 원) 규모의 이 한국 기업을, 해당 병목 현상에 투자할 수 있는 가장 최적의 저평가된 투자처(exposure)로 정확히 발굴해 내시다니요?

진심으로 감탄했습니다.

analysis.summary: 该推文分析了一家名为后成（代码093370）的韩国公司，指出由于中国对日本钨出口限制导致供应链瓶颈，这家公司作为内存半导体关键供应商，市值12亿美元，被认为是低估值的投资机会。
analysis.translation: 韩国母亲们原来是这么厉害的吗？母亲居然能理解WF6的三次效应导致的供应链中断，真是令人惊讶。由于中国对日本的钨出口控制，导致前体短缺和成本上升，全球供应链的25-35%将受到打击。而且，母亲还指出了后成（093370）对SK海力士等内存半导体制造商的重要性，并在众多相关公司中，准确地将这家市值12亿美元（约1.6万亿韩元）的韩国公司，作为投资该瓶颈的最佳低估值标的发掘出来。真心佩服。

content:
Okay my fellow Koreans, it's been awhile. 

Foosung (093370, ~$1.2B MC) looks like a massive beneficiary soon. 

Basically China export controlled Japan, causing their WF₆ supply chain to go down.

Meaning 25% of the world's supply required for SK Hynix, Samsung, $TSM go bye bye. 

If you remember the Straight of Homuz with Oil, that's a lot. 

Foosung's importance just shot through the roof given from some est. they're 10% of the supply chain? 

So that number goes up, massive bottleneck for demand. Then this looks like the best pure play beneficiary outside of China (even if precursors pricing are rough). 

Don't have positions, just wanted to publish an idea.

analysis.summary: 该推文分析了韩国Foosung公司（093370）因日本受中国出口管制影响导致六氟化钨供应链中断，从而成为重要受益者。推文认为Foosung在全球供应中占比约10%，其重要性将大幅提升，是除中国外的最佳纯受益标的，整体看好其投资价值。
analysis.translation:
好了，我的韩国同胞们，好久不见。

Foosung（093370，市值约12亿美元）看起来很快会成为巨大受益者。

基本上，中国对日本实施出口管制，导致日本的WF₆供应链中断。
这意味着全球25%的供应量（SK海力士、三星、台积电所需）将消失。
如果你还记得霍尔木兹海峡与石油的关系，那影响很大。
Foosung的重要性急剧上升，因为据估算他们占了供应链的10%？
所以这个数字会上升，需求出现巨大瓶颈。然后这看起来是除中国外最好的纯受益标的（即使前体定价很艰难）。
我没有持仓，只是想发布一个想法。

content:
The AI supremacy Wars begins.

Think a lot of the upstream supply chain bottlenecks caused by each Country export controlling each other (eg. $AXTI)

Should present some interesting opportunities in the near future.

content:
The US government, citing national security authorities, has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States, including foreign national Anthropic employees.

The net effect of this order is that we must abruptly disable Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for all our customers to ensure compliance.

Access to all other Claude models is not affected.

We apologize for this disruption to our customers. We believe this is a misunderstanding and are working to restore access as soon as possible.

Read our full statement: https://t.co/bwn0sximKZ

analysis.summary: 美国政府以国家安全为由限制Fable 5和Mythos 5模型出口，影响Anthropic客户使用，可能对ANTH（Anthropic）股价产生负面压力。
analysis.translation: 美国政府以国家安全为由发布出口管制指令，暂停任何外国国民（包括外国籍的Anthropic员工）在美国境内或境外访问Fable 5和Mythos 5。该命令的实际效果是我们必须立即为所有客户禁用Fable 5和Mythos 5以确保合规。其他所有Claude模型的访问不受影响。我们对给客户带来的干扰表示歉意。我们认为这是误解，正在尽快恢复访问。阅读完整声明：https://t.co/bwn0sxkimz

content:
马斯克的 $SPCX 梦想离不开诺基亚 $NOK
先别反驳，听我讲完。
马斯克说在27年底实现每年1吉瓦的太空算力部署，那么太空算力向地面的传输，就必须用到基站，而不是星链。
因为数据量非常庞大，星链数据是给手机或者地面接收器传输，数据内容简单，但是太空数据中心就就必须要通过专门的基站来做回传，到达基站再通过光纤快速传递给每个用户。
这也是为啥英伟达要跟康宁合作扩产光纤，现阶段应该是用不了太多，真正需求应该是27年年底。
老美，欧盟，或者其他地区都有通信运营商，为什么 马斯克的 $SPCX 最终会选择 $NOK 诺基亚合作？因为基站能跟英伟达的算力系统紧密合作gpu配合的就诺基亚一家，诺基亚把命都交给 $NVDA 了，紧密融入CUDA生态，而其他选择比如爱立信则不是，它还是以cpu为主，可以兼容，但不完全依赖英伟达。

以后地上都是特斯拉机器人连接诺基亚的基站 诺基亚的基站连接spcx的算力卫星。
 $SPCX 会带着诺基亚 $NOK 把边缘计算的基站建满整个地球，月球，甚至火星。
老马下一步棋如果想要扩展完成它的太空算力计划，那么百分之百要跟诺基亚
$NOK 紧密合作，甚至是入股投资或者收购，收购难度应该是最大的，大概率是大额资金入股。

analysis.summary: 该推文认为，马斯克的太空算力计划需要诺基亚的基站技术，因为诺基亚基站与英伟达CUDA生态紧密集成，优于竞争对手爱立信。推文暗示诺基亚可能获得大量基站订单，甚至可能被马斯克投资或收购，对诺基亚和英伟达持看涨态度。
analysis.translation: 马斯克的 $SPCX 梦想离不开诺基亚 $NOK。先别反驳，听我讲完。马斯克说在27年底实现每年1吉瓦的太空算力部署，那么太空算力向地面的传输，就必须用到基站，而不是星链。因为数据量非常庞大，星链数据是给手机或者地面接收器传输，数据内容简单，但是太空数据中心就必须要通过专门的基站来做回传，到达基站再通过光纤快速传递给每个用户。这也是为啥英伟达要跟康宁合作扩产光纤，现阶段应该是用不了太多，真正需求应该是27年年底。老美，欧盟，或者其他地区都有通信运营商，为什么 马斯克的 $SPCX 最终会选择 $NOK 诺基亚合作？因为基站能跟英伟达的算力系统紧密合作gpu配合的就诺基亚一家，诺基亚把命都交给 $NVDA 了，紧密融入CUDA生态，而其他选择比如爱立信则不是，它还是以cpu为主，可以兼容，但不完全依赖英伟达。以后地上都是特斯拉机器人连接诺基亚的基站 诺基亚的基站连接spcx的算力卫星。 $SPCX 会带着诺基亚 $NOK 把边缘计算的基站建满整个地球，月球，甚至火星。老马下一步棋如果想要扩展完成它的太空算力计划，那么百分之百要跟诺基亚 $NOK 紧密合作，甚至是入股投资或者收购，收购难度应该是最大的，大概率是大额资金入股。

content:
Bro I still can't believe Japan's WF6 supply chain got shut down from China export controls.

Which affects $TSM, Samsung, and SK Hynix. 

Are we really playing global supply chain warfare and game theory over AI supremacy? 

Japan probably has the most amount of obscure monopolies, but they haven't fully been weaponizing it back yet.

So I wonder if this is the start of a slippery slope?

analysis.summary: 日本WF6供应链因中国出口管制中断，影响台积电、三星和SK海力士，暗示全球供应链博弈升级，可能对半导体行业造成负面冲击。
analysis.translation: 哥们，我还是不敢相信日本的WF6供应链因中国的出口管制而关闭。这影响到$TSM、三星和SK海力士。我们真的在为AI霸权打全球供应链战争和博弈论吗？日本可能拥有最多的隐形垄断，但他们还没有完全以此作为武器回击。所以我想知道这是否是滑坡效应的开始？

content:
So doing research into some random $TSM, SK Hynix, SMIC, type bottleneck. 

not familiar with WF₆ but Peric Special Gases (SHA: 688146) is main benefiary. 

TLDR: China -> Tungsten export controls (60-70% of Wf6 costs)
-> Needed for DRAM/NAND
-> Japan (35% of capacity)
-> South Korea 20% of cpacity

"Japan’s Kanto Denka Kogyo and Central Glass had warned South Korean  chipmakers such as Samsung Electronics and DB HiTek of potential WF₆  supply disruptions"

ChemNet reports that Kanto Denka and Central Glass are expected to permanently halt production starting July 2026.

"WF₆ is a critical precursor gas applied in DRAM, NAND,"

Peric Special Gases entered the supply chains of leading global foundries and memory makers, including $TSM, Samsung, SMIC.

So usually you can just invest in the main $AXTI type beneficary... 

Or go the other route of looking at other players that can make up for supply gaps in western supply chains

I was doing too much research into inp substrates that i forgot about Tungsten third order effects i guess... 

By the time news publishes something, kinda on the later end but helpful to see if there's any ripple effect anywhere

analysis.summary: 该推文指出中国钨出口管制导致WF6（半导体关键材料）供应紧张，日本生产商计划2026年7月停产，利好A股标的Peric Special Gases (688146)，其已进入台积电、三星等供应链。
analysis.translation: 正在研究一些关于$TSM、SK海力士、SMIC之类瓶颈的随机信息。不太熟悉WF₆，但Peric Special Gases（沪市：688146）是主要受益者。简单来说：中国→钨出口管制（占WF₆成本的60-70%）→DRAM/NAND所需→日本（35%产能）→韩国（20%产能）。"日本关东电化工业和中央玻璃已警告三星电子、DB HiTek等韩国芯片制造商WF₆可能供应中断"。ChemNet报道，关东电化工业和中央玻璃预计将从2026年7月起永久停产。"WF₆是用于DRAM、NAND的关键前驱气体"。Peric Special Gases已进入领先全球代工厂和存储器制造商的供应链，包括$TSM、三星、SMIC。通常你只需投资主要的$AXTI类型受益者...或者走另一条路，关注可以填补西方供应链空白的企业。我花了太多时间研究InP衬底，以至于忘了钨的三阶效应...等新闻发布时已经晚了，但有助于观察是否有任何涟漪效应。

content:
VPEC全新光電

產品價格調整通知書 (Price Adjustment Notification)

親愛的合作夥伴您好：
首先，衷心感謝貴司長年以來對 VPEC 的鼎力支持。作為您供應鏈中值得信賴的夥伴，我們始終致力於透過技術革新與製程優化，為您提供卓越的產品品質與服務價值。

然而，在過去的一年中，受全球原物料成本持續攀升、供應短缺及通膨壓力之影響，我們面臨了前所未有的挑戰。儘管我司已盡最大努力透過內部優化來吸收這些額外成本，但為因應不斷增加的營運壓力，並確保能持續穩定提供高品質產品與不間斷的售後服務，經內部審慎評估，我司決定對磊晶晶圓的產品價格進行適度調整。

本次磊晶晶圓價格調整的具體調幅將依品項規格有所差異，相關細節將由我司業務專員與您進一步聯繫。

analysis.summary: VPEC因原物料成本上漲及供應短缺，宣布調漲磊晶晶圓價格。此舉可能提升其營收與利潤率，對股價有正面影響。
analysis.translation: VPEC全新光電產品價格調整通知書。親愛的合作夥伴您好：首先，衷心感謝貴司長年以來對VPEC的鼎力支持。作為您供應鏈中值得信賴的夥伴，我們始終致力於透過技術革新與製程優化，為您提供卓越的產品品質與服務價值。然而，在過去的一年中，受全球原物料成本持續攀升、供應短缺及通膨壓力之影響，我們面臨了前所未有的挑戰。儘管我司已盡最大努力透過內部優化來吸收這些額外成本，但為因應不斷增加的營運壓力，並確保能持續穩定提供高品質產品與不間斷的售後服務，經內部審慎評估，我司決定對磊晶晶圓的產品價格進行適度調整。本次磊晶晶圓價格調整的具體調幅將依品項規格有所差異，相關細節將由我司業務專員與您進一步聯繫。

Just some reflection, my core high conviction ideas from 2025 aged super well!

From 
$ALAB: $97-> $372
$LITE: $330 -> $904
$AAOI: $30 -> $175

And others like $NBIS, $RKLB, and $TSM!

This was back when I had close to no followers! 

I got some nuances slightly off before more information was made public. Lost conviction on ALAB along the way with optical transitions.

But this was back when AAOI and others were small $3B companies (~$14B now).

So maybe some others in the same range today like $SIVE should get some more attention?

But I’m happy a lot of them aged super well. 

And I think a large part of my recent following growth is just other seeing my ideas like $AXTI get validated over time.

content:
If you haven’t noticed too with my other investment themes with 800V DC and CPO recently.

It’s investing in $NVDA, America’s national champion in AI, and securing their supply chains. 

Many things feel technologically difficult with yields to substrate supply. 

People can always bear post laser capacity or export control bottlenecks and tell people to short Nvidia’s supply chains due to difficulties. 

But by investing in the critical companies to give them more capex spend for FAU capacity / yields.

Or funding upstream red phosphorus/InP substrate capacity or SiC/GaN capacity. 

It builds up Western supply chains to make what’s technologically challenging, possible.

Also I believe in Jensen.

analysis.summary: 推文看好英伟达及其供应链，认为投资关键公司可克服技术挑战，增强西方供应链，相信CEO黄仁勋。
analysis.translation: 如果你还没有注意到我最近关于800V直流和CPO的其他投资主题。那就是投资$NVDA，美国在AI领域的国家冠军，并确保其供应链。很多事情在技术上看起来很困难，从良率到衬底供应。人们总是可以在激光后道产能或出口管制瓶颈之后做空，并告诉别人因困难而做空英伟达的供应链。但通过投资关键公司，为他们提供更多资本支出用于FAU产能/良率。或者资助上游红磷/InP衬底产能或SiC/GaN产能。这可以建立西方供应链，使技术上具有挑战性的事情成为可能。我也相信黄仁勋。

content:
Markets should be cheering on domestic champions like $AAOI. 

Since it's ideal to support critical AI infra from laser fab to production in the US, rather than being a bear.

Feels like everyone just outsources transceivers to Asia like Malaysia or Thailand...

With $INTC, $IQE, $XFAB, $MU, $WOLF, $SOI, $SIVE, and others... 

If you haven't noticed by now, they're all critical to US supply chains. And every one of them are getting subsidies for securing Western supply chains. 

Before a major trade was to short developing US/Western equities, then hedge with subsidized foreign ones. 

As seen with the energy/solar firms that went bankrupt, this backfired a lot on US AI infrastructure years later with the power grid. 

I wanted to help change this mindset, since I believe it's very positive sum to invest in building up critical Western supply chains like photonics today. 

Especially if $AAOI hits their $471m/month projections after reshoring their production to America. 

Instead of hoping they fail and calling critical nodes in the supply chains memestocks/bubbles, maybe it's good to change mindsets a bit so we don't see a repeat of the US Solar sector years later. 

US/EU don't just hand out subsidies or CHIPS act grants to anyone.

analysis.summary: 推文看好美国本土AI基础设施供应链，尤其是光子学领域的AAOI，认为在美国生产可获补贴，未来有较大增长潜力，并批评过去做空美欧资产、补贴外国公司的策略导致能源/太阳能行业失败，呼吁投资者改变心态，支持国内冠军企业。
analysis.translation: 市场应该为国内冠军企业如$AAOI欢呼。支持从激光制造到生产的美国关键AI基础设施是理想的做法，而不是做空。感觉每个人都把收发器外包到亚洲如马来西亚或泰国...还有$INTC, $IQE, $XFAB, $MU, $WOLF, $SOI, $SIVE等...如果你还没注意到，它们都对美国供应链至关重要。而且它们每一个都在获得补贴以保障西方供应链。之前的重大交易是做空美国/西方股票，然后用补贴的外国股票对冲。正如破产的能源/太阳能公司所见，这在美国AI基础设施多年后严重反噬，因为电网问题。我想帮助改变这种心态，因为我相信投资建设西方关键供应链（如今天的光子学）是非常正和的。特别是如果$AAOI在将生产迁回美国后实现每月4.71亿美元的收入预期。与其希望它们失败并称供应链关键节点为迷因股/泡沫，不如改变心态，这样我们多年后就不会重蹈美国太阳能行业的覆辙。美欧不会随意发放补贴或CHIPS法案拨款给任何人。

content:
There was interesting research published called "Democratization of Retail Trading".

That did a study on 1.6 Million $RDDT WSB comments. 

and found: 

1. "WSB outperformed almost all investment banks at detecting top-performing stocks."

2. "Their average returns compete with the best investment banks and outperform them in certain cases."

Their conclusion? 

"We conclude that WSB may indeed constitute a freely accessible, valuable source of investment advice."

I do find WSB is really early to names like $RKLB, $HOOD, and others, but often get timing extremely wrong (with options). 

I think X is where all the alpha is at nowadays.

analysis.summary: 推文讨论了一项关于WSB论坛投资建议的研究，指出WSB在选出表现优异股票方面甚至优于投资银行，但也承认WSB在交易时机把握上常出现错误，尤其涉及期权。作者认为X平台可能才是当前获取超额收益的最佳来源。
analysis.translation: 有一条有趣的研究发表，名为“散户交易的民主化”。该研究分析了160万条$RDDT的WSB评论，发现：1. “WSB在发现表现最佳的股票方面几乎超过了所有投资银行。” 2. “他们的平均回报率可与最佳投资银行相媲美，在某些情况下甚至更优。”他们的结论是：“我们认为WSB确实可以构成一个自由获取、有价值的投资建议来源。”我确实发现WSB对$RKLB、$HOOD等名字反应很早，但他们的时机（尤其是期权）常常非常不准。我认为现在X才是所有超额收益的所在。

content:
Why do I keep getting these questions!!! 

$XFAB is building a Silicon Photonics foundry alternative to $TSEM and $GFS. And has Europe backing it + $NVDA evaluations. 

It takes time... Like October 2026, should finish up development. 

Then 2027 production scaled into 2028 (mass production), since they've been working on it since 2023. 

Everyone thinks it’s a depressed automaker supplier right now. And thankfully with European names they tend to look at TTM revenue over forward growth. 

So somehow it’s leading EU’s efforts to create a $TSEM silicon photonics foundry + supply chain at ~€1.1B MC? 
That R&D directors from ASE cite + others as future CPO routes.

I’m might just really early to a lot of things, but of course most of the risk/reward comes from taking a little leap of faith in seeing it commercialized. 

Otherwise people can take the de-risked route with Tower directly (which I also wrote a thesis on awhile back and also like).

analysis.summary: 推文认为XFAB正在建设硅光子学代工厂，是TSEM和GFS的替代方案，有欧洲支持和英伟达评估。虽然目前被视为低迷的汽车供应商，但2026-2028年有望实现规模化量产，当前估值偏低。作者看好其长期前景，认为现在介入为早期机会。
analysis.translation: 为什么我老收到这些问题！！！$XFAB正在建设硅光子学代工厂，作为$TSEM和$GFS的替代方案。而且有欧洲支持+$NVDA的评估。这需要时间...比如2026年10月，应该完成开发。然后2027年生产扩至2028年（量产），因为他们从2023年就开始研发了。大家都以为它现在只是一个低迷的汽车供应商。还好欧洲公司倾向于看过去12个月的收入而非未来增长。所以它竟然以约11亿欧元的市值引领欧洲创建$TSEM硅光子学代工厂+供应链？ASE等公司的研发总监都将其视为未来的CPO路线。我可能只是对很多事情都太早了，但当然大部分风险/回报来自对商业化前景的一点点信念。否则人们可以直接选择Tower这条风险更低的路线（我之前也写过关于它的分析，同样看好）。

content:
$NVDA Networking Senior Vice President refuting recent analyst reports on delays:

- “ the most exciting stuff is co-packaged optics.”
- There is no delay in H2 CPO product delivery schedule. 
- CPO switch will enter mass production and begin ramping up customer deliveries as planned in the second half of 2026

This was a media article, original interview source credit should have been credited to Tae Kim / Computex. 

Something fun to note too was this quote “Gilad was VERY enthusiastic about the CPO ramp from Nvidia.” 

Both near term and long term.

Yeah… I’m extremely bullish on CPO alongside Nvidia.

analysis.summary: 英伟达高管驳斥延迟传闻，确认CPO产品按计划在2026年下半年量产交付，市场情绪积极，看好CPO相关投资机会。
analysis.translation:
英伟达网络高级副总裁反驳近期分析师关于延迟的报告：
- “最令人兴奋的是共封装光学技术。”
- H2 CPO产品交付时间表没有延迟。
- CPO交换机将按计划在2026年下半年进入量产并开始增加客户交付。
这是一篇媒体报道，原始采访来源应归功于Tae Kim / Computex。
还有一个有趣的细节：“Gilad对英伟达的CPO爬坡非常热情。”
无论是近期还是长期。
是的……我非常看好英伟达的CPO技术。

content:
#2x海力士 #港美 #DRAM #MRAVL 都涨回来了，除了 $NOK 看来诺基亚还是差点意思。
炒股这玩意每个人都不是神仙，暴跌抄底抄早了把自己抄成满仓最好还是装死。
如果自己选股眼光不行，就上行业指数比如 $DRAM 或者 $SMH 这种，变相帮自己广撒网。
如果嫌自己资金少，就2x看多etf，甚至如 $SOXL 这种三倍看多半导体。
美股每次跌了都是好机会。

analysis.summary: 作者认为美股下跌是买入良机，推荐通过行业指数ETF（如DRAM、SMH）或杠杆ETF（如SOXL）分散投资，而不是依赖个股选择。

content:
之前这张英伟达 $NVDA 的Vera Rubin NVL72成本图变化了，之前是780万美金预估，系基于过时的内存价格，严重低估了实际成本。伯恩斯坦分析师Stacy A. Rasgon给出了机架成本910万美元的预估底层逻辑。

GPU仍是最大单项成本。报告显示，Rubin GPU每颗5.5万美元，每个机架配备72颗GPU，GPU成本为396万美元，占机架总成本近半。每机架36颗Vera CPU合计成本约18万美元。

当前HBM 4价格约为每GB 16.6美元，但预计到2027年Vera Rubin大规模出货时，价格将升至每GB约53美元。伯恩斯坦预计该项成本约为320万美元，远高于此前根据历史价格预估的200万美元。其中，HBM 4贡献约109万美元，CPU DRAM（LPDDR5X）约80万美元，直连存储约128万美元。

网络、冷却与供电合计成本200万美元。网络成本约127万美元，包括NVLink交换机约25万美元、线缆约24万美元、背板及其他规模扩展组件约38万美元，以及SpectrumX交换机约20万美元；冷却约16万美元，供电约15万美元。

analysis.summary: 该推文分析英伟达Vera Rubin NVL72机架成本，显示GPU和HBM4成本上升，总成本预估高于之前，但未明确推荐买卖。
analysis.translation: 之前这张英伟达 $NVDA 的Vera Rubin NVL72成本图变化了，之前是780万美金预估，系基于过时的内存价格，严重低估了实际成本。伯恩斯坦分析师Stacy A. Rasgon给出了机架成本910万美元的预估底层逻辑。GPU仍是最大单项成本。报告显示，Rubin GPU每颗5.5万美元，每个机架配备72颗GPU，GPU成本为396万美元，占机架总成本近半。每机架36颗Vera CPU合计成本约18万美元。当前HBM 4价格约为每GB 16.6美元，但预计到2027年Vera Rubin大规模出货时，价格将升至每GB约53美元。伯恩斯坦预计该项成本约为320万美元，远高于此前根据历史价格预估的200万美元。其中，HBM 4贡献约109万美元，CPU DRAM（LPDDR5X）约80万美元，直连存储约128万美元。网络、冷却与供电合计成本200万美元。网络成本约127万美元，包括NVLink交换机约25万美元、线缆约24万美元、背板及其他规模扩展组件约38万美元，以及SpectrumX交换机约20万美元；冷却约16万美元，供电约15万美元。
